docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• To provide a sensitive and caring response to family members.
  • To permanently close the student's academic records.
  • To follow a uniform procedure that properly informs all departments/staff listed below.

docs.title: 1. Initial Notification
docs.text: When a staff or faculty member is notified of a student's death, they will notify the Registrar/Associate Dean of Admissions.

docs.title: 2. Obtain Documentation
docs.text: Information about the student is obtained from family, faculty, counselor, or other person. Obtain proof of death (documentation can be a newspaper obituary or article). The sheet and documentation will be scanned and placed in the student's file.

docs.title: 3. Registrar/Associate Dean Notifications
docs.text:
The Registrar/Associate Dean within Admissions will contact the following staff via email, phone call, or personal visit:

Contact Purpose College President and Executive Secretary Mails a letter of condolence Vice President of Instruction and Student Services For information only Faculty Notification if student is active in classes Financial Aid Check if the student was receiving aid Special Programs (EOPS, DSPS) For coordination Graduation Office Check if a petition is on file Cashier's Office Check if the student has outstanding fees Associate Dean of Student Development For information only Communications Specialist, Public Affairs If media becomes involved Psychological Services If counseling and support may be needed Scholarship Office Check if student is receiving a monetary award Human Resources or Job Placement In case student was employed within the district

docs.title: 4. Colleague Updates (Datatel)
docs.text:
Admissions will perform the following in Colleague/Datatel:

  • NAE: Type DECEASED in the address line.
  • NAE: Add the mail code DE to eliminate the possibility of mailing to this person.
  • NAE: Remove the email address.
  • BIO: Change the Directory field to N (no) to exclude from FERPA releases.
  • BIO: Enter Deceased in the status field.
  • PERC: Place a deceased hold on the student's record.
  • DEC: Enter deceased student data.
  • Check for hard copies of transcripts. Request SAC to attach a note stating DECEASED on the hard copy.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
Danger

Do not release any information on a deceased student without a subpoena.

Family Transcript Requests

Sometimes family members request the student's transcript. They must show a copy of the death certificate and ID to identify their relationship to the student.

docs.title: Subpoenas
docs.text:
Needs to Be Reviewed for Updates

This procedure may need to be reviewed for any policy updates.

docs.title: 1. Notice to Student
docs.text:
• If a Release of Authorization form is attached to the subpoena, no mailed notice is required.
  • If no release is attached:
    1. Prepare the Letter and Notice to Consumer or Employee.
    2. Place the notice in an envelope and mail to the student using:
      • Certified Mail
      • Return Receipt Requested
    3. Deliver the prepared envelope and certified mail forms to Bee in the Mail Room.
    4. Upon receipt:
      • Attach the returned receipt to the filed subpoena.
      • If the mail is returned, attach the returned mail to the filed subpoena as well.
  • Wait 7 calendar days for a response.
  • If no response is received after 7 days, begin processing the subpoena.

docs.title: 2. Review and Distribution
docs.text:
1. Review the subpoena to determine what records are being requested.
  2. Forward the subpoena to all applicable departments to gather the required information.
  3. Compile all received documentation.
  4. Send completed records and documents to Risk Management.

docs.title: A. Application Records
docs.text:
• LaserFiche — Print the student's application.
  • OpenCCCApply Report Center — Print the student's application.

docs.title: B. Transcripts (TRAN)
docs.text:
1. Enter the Student ID.
  2. Save.
  3. P → FormFusion
  4. Use the printing code.
  5. Save and update.

docs.title: C. Other Student Records (If Requested)
docs.text: Copy all relevant correspondence and student records including instructor rosters, grades, and programs. Ensure all documents are in order and legible. Only include the requested student's records. Redact or white out other students' information as needed.

docs.title: D. Full Grades and Rosters (If Required)
docs.text:
1. STAC — Print course list.
  2. M: Drive → Instructor Grades:
    • Search by semester.
    • Search by instructor and section.
    • Locate rosters for each registered class.
  3. Redact or black out all other students' information.
  4. Convert all documents to PDF and ensure redactions are applied correctly.

docs.title: 4. Subpoenas Requiring Financial Information
docs.text:
Contact the appropriate offices for financial records:

  • CO/Auxiliary Services — Jennie Adams, Ext. 46433
  • Financial Aid — Thuy Huong Nguyen, Ext. 46485

Email the department(s) with the subpoena request and student name/ID. Request that documentation be sent via interoffice mail if applicable.

docs.title: 5. Subpoena Fees Form
docs.text:
• Track all time spent processing the subpoena for billing purposes.
  • Copy the tracked time from Excel and paste it as an image into a letterhead document.
  • Save the file as Subpoena Fees.
  • Convert the document to PDF by right-clicking on the file.

docs.title: 6. Custodian of Records Form
docs.text:
• Complete the Custodian of Records form provided by HR.
  • Attach the completed form to the subpoena package.

docs.title: Document Order
docs.text:
1. Custodian of Records Form
  2. Subpoena Fees Form
  3. Application
  4. Transcripts
  5. Additional subpoena-requested documents

docs.title: Distribution and Filing
docs.text:
• Make copies of all documents.
  • Email the completed subpoena package to Annina Brown.
  • File physical copies in the first drawer located by Mark's office.
  • Include the notification letter to the student in the file.
    • If the letter is returned, file it with the subpoena documentation.

docs.title: 8. Special Notes and Exceptions
docs.text:
Public Defender Subpoenas

Do not pay the $15 fee.

Workers' Compensation Subpoenas

  • Include application and transcripts only.
  • Send documents to the District.
  • No fee letter is required.
  • Print official transcripts from TRAN.

docs.title: AB 540 — California Nonresident Tuition Exemption
docs.text: Tags: AB 540 · Residency · Non-Resident Tuition Exemption · ARI

docs.title: ScribeHow Guide
docs.text: How to process AB 540 (ScribeHow)

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: AB 540 allows certain non-resident students to pay in-state tuition rates if they meet specific criteria — primarily having attended a California high school for 3 or more years and graduated (or equivalent).

docs.title: Processing Steps
docs.text:
1. Verify the student has submitted a completed California Nonresident Tuition Exemption Request (AB 540 Affidavit).
  2. Confirm the student attended a California high school for 3 or more years and graduated (or equivalent). Check transcripts or diploma on file.

  3. Open Colleague → ASPR screen.

    Field What to Enter Residency Status Insert a new line and residency code of A Date One day before the start of the desired term Comments/Remarks Timestamp and make notes

  4. Scan the signed affidavit and attach to the student's record in Laserfiche / Perceptive Content.

  5. Notify the student that the exemption has been applied and advise them to re-check their fee balance.

docs.title: Students Who Forgot to Turn In a Form
docs.text:
Warning

If a student forgot to submit the affidavit before the fee deadline, accept the late form and process it. Apply the updated AB 540 status on ASPR, then route to the Cashier's Office for adjustment.

docs.title: Students Switching to CA Resident
docs.text: When a student establishes California residency (after one year + intent to remain), change their classification from AB 540 exemption to CA Resident in Colleague. Insert a new line with the R status. Document the residency reclassification date and make notes.

docs.title: Students Who Were Homeschooled
docs.text: Homeschooled students may qualify if they were homeschooled in California for 3+ years. Request an affidavit from a parent or guardian attesting to homeschool instruction in California. Treat as equivalent to high school attendance.

docs.title: Students Who Are Not Living in CA
docs.text:
Long distance learning students who are residing out of state do not qualify for AB 540.

Info

“Please also be aware that the Chancellor's Office has determined that the AB 540 exemption from Nonresident Tuition (see item 23, above) is not available for persons who are absent from California, but who are taking distance education courses from California Community Colleges (Cal. Code Regs. tit. 5, § 54045.5; CCCCO Residency Overview, 2025).”

docs.title: Students That List Continuing Education as School
docs.text: AB 540 is a self-certified affidavit. We should process these based on the student's statement of truth. Verification is only required if there is a discrepancy between the ASUM records and the information provided on the form.

docs.title: General Guidelines
docs.text:
Always verify information before processing.

  • Everyone must pay the $2 fee, except for:

    • Loan deferrals
    • Investigative requests for educational record data
    • GPA verification letters

  • FERPA Compliance: Per Mark, if the request involves information beyond directory information, we need a Release Authorization Waiver on file. To be safe, always request a signed waiver and a copy of the student's photo ID.

  • Processing Timelines: Ask the student which semester the request is for. Verification forms for current or upcoming semesters can be processed per Mark — just remember to put the last date for refund for the latest class.

docs.title: Verification Letters
docs.text:
When a student needs us to provide a enrollment verification letter for them.

Fee: $2 at the Cashier's Office (no charge for SAC's Childcare program).

  1. Confirm payment (initial receipt required).
  2. Complete or generate the verification letter.
  3. Write N/A in any areas that are Not Applicable.
  4. Stamp with Mark's signature.
  5. Crimp and seal the letter.
  6. Use STAC or RGN screens to confirm unit load:
    • Full-time:
      • Spring/Fall: 12+ units
      • Summer/Intersession: 6+ units
    • Part-time:
      • Spring/Fall: 11 or fewer units
      • Summer/Intersession: 5 or fewer units
  7. If it's a special case, direct the student to the Cashier's Office to pay.
  8. Student must email admissions@sac.edu with:
    • Full name, SAC ID, birthdate, last 4 digits of SSN
    • Reason for verification
    • Cashier's receipt (attached)

docs.title: Verification Forms
docs.text:
When a student provides a form for us to complete.

Fee: $2 at the Cashier's Office (no charge for SAC's Childcare program).

Cannot process until the 3rd week of the semester or after the refund/drop deadline.

  1. Check class details to confirm drop date.
  2. Complete the form.
  3. If needed, send to other departments via interoffice mail for completion.
  4. Stamp with Mark's signature and crimp.
  5. If student has a deadline, attach a verification letter explaining the drop date hasn't passed.
  6. Staple the letter to the original form.

docs.title: Additional Notes
docs.text:
• We do not verify information over the phone.
    • If a 3rd party calls, refer to National Student Clearinghouse.
  • When emailing or responding to schools/employers: direct to Clearinghouse when possible, or collect $2 for form completion.
  • FERPA-Blocked Students: If an employer provides a valid release form, verify identity and proceed.
  • Email processing is allowed without the embosser (per Chris) if needed.
  • Always encrypt emailed letters so they can't be edited.
  • For students who only attended SCC (not SAC): issue a letter clarifying SAC and SCC are part of the same district and state the student attended the district but only enrolled in SCC courses.
  • If in doubt, request a signed release authorization to ensure FERPA compliance.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) protects the privacy of student records by prohibiting the disclosure of personal information without express consent. To ensure legal compliance, staff must obtain a signed FERPA Release before sharing any non-directory data with third parties.

Students can submit FERPA Release Requests through https://sac.edu/ferpa.

docs.title: Entering a FERPA Release Request on Colleague
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Colleague → PERC.
  2. Enter the Student ID and press Enter.
  3. Insert a new line by clicking on the 1 button and click Insert.
  4. Enter 1FEPA in the empty line.
  5. Timestamp the entry.
  6. Make comments about:
    • Who the student is releasing information to
    • What information they would like released
  7. Click Save All.

docs.title: Releasing Information to Someone Other Than the Student
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Colleague → PERC.
  2. Check to see if there is a 1FEPA hold.
  3. Detail into the hold.
  4. Verify that the requestor matches the 1FEPA comments by asking for ID.
  5. Release only the information specified in the comments.
  6. If there is no 1FEPA in PERC, navigate to Colleague → BIO.
  7. Check if Directory Information has a Y.
  8. If Y, then we can release directory information (more information below). If N, we cannot release directory information.

docs.title: Directory Information
docs.text:
Under FERPA at the college level, directory information is non-sensitive data, such as name, address, phone number, email, dates of attendance, major, enrollment status (full/part-time), and degrees/awards. Institutions may release this information without consent unless a student opts out, but it is not considered an invasion of privacy.

Common College Directory Information Includes:

  • Name, address, telephone number, and email address.
  • Date and place of birth.
  • Major field of study, class standing, and academic level (e.g., undergraduate or graduate).
  • Dates of attendance and enrollment status (e.g., full-time or part-time).
  • Degrees, honors, and awards received.
  • Participation in officially recognized activities and sports.
  • Photographs.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text: Note Description Opt-Out Right: Students have the right to restrict the release of this information by filing a "FERPA block" or privacy request with the registrar. Not Mandatory Release: Institutions are not required to release directory information; they may do so, but are not obligated to. What is NOT Included: Social security numbers, student ID numbers (if usable to access records directly), grades, GPA, and disciplinary records are strictly protected and never considered public directory information.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Financial Aid office issues High School Status Discrepancy forms when a student's high school data in Colleague does not align with the information provided on their FAFSA.

docs.title: Processing Steps
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Colleague → IASU.
  2. Detail into the student's high school.

  3. Verify if we have the correct high school graduation status.

    • If it is correct, the student needs to update their FAFSA application. Once it is updated, the student needs to notify our Financial Aid office.

    • If it is incorrect, proceed to the following steps.

  4. Request the student's official high school diploma or official high school transcript showing awarding of diploma.

    • Make copies.
    • Send originals to scanning.

  5. Open IASU or ASUM.

    • Drill into High School or adult education center.
    • Find Graduation Type and update as appropriate:
    Graduation Type What It Means Diploma High school diploma from a school; will be on their official transcript Proficiency Exam Equivalent to GED High School Equivalency GED per Financial Aid HiSET GED GED State-issued GED certificate Foreign Diploma Completed high school in another country

  6. Click Save All.

  7. Mark the form and return it to the student to turn into Financial Aid.
  8. We can archive the Financial Aid form instead of having the student fill out a new Change of Information Request.

docs.title: Student Completed High School in Another Country
docs.text:
• Change to Foreign Diploma.
  • We do not need to ask for documentation.

docs.title: If It Is an Old Form
docs.text:
• The form displays the term it is referencing.
  • If it is for a past term, students no longer need it.
  • Have them contact Financial Aid.

docs.title: Can We Use Unofficial Transcripts?
docs.text:
• We can use unofficial transcripts per Hung.

From H. Nguyen, 12/30/21

For the high school transcript I would update it since it is self-reported on CCCApply. However, in order for us to remove the bachelor's degree we would need proof of non-enrollment/degree because it may make the student eligible or ineligible for financial aid.

docs.title: Student Cannot Obtain High School Transcripts or Diploma
docs.text:
• Case-by-case review.
  • Check w/ Dean/Associate Dean.

docs.title: After Receiving Letter of Non-Enrollment for University
docs.text: • Financial Aid will take over and have the student sign a form stating the student made a mistake in reporting they received a bachelor's degree, and they will no longer be able to transfer units from that program to SAC.

docs.title: Diploma vs. GED — Full Explanation
docs.text:
From Sarah Salas

High school diploma is different from the GED. High School diploma is a degree program where students complete a minimum of 160 credits. The record will be on their official transcript. GED/HiSET is an aptitude test or an equivalency to the High School diploma — students take a state (California) test and pass or fail based on their score. There are 3 portions to the test and a fee is associated with it. Completion of the GED/HiSET equivalency test is not recorded in their transcript since they are taking the test issued by the state. Students who complete the test get their result directly from the state GED/HiSET office.

  • GEDs are not posted on high school diplomas.

docs.title: 1. Form Completion (Paper)
docs.text:
Ensure the Petition for Overlapping Classes form is fully completed and includes all required signatures:

  • Student
  • Instructor of the overlapping course
  • Division Dean
  • Vice President of Academic Affairs

docs.title: 2. Instructor Responsibilities
docs.text:
The overlapping class is the one where the instructor is allowing the student to arrive late or leave early. That instructor must:

  • Track and log the student's hours.
  • Submit a completed time sheet by the end of the semester.

docs.title: 3. Add/Registration Process
docs.text:
1. Add the course through RGN.
  2. If the class is full and the student is waitlisted, you may override the waitlist if:
    • The class has started.
    • Instructor permission is already on the Overlap Petition itself.
  3. Detail into course on STAC.
    • Detail into Notes/Comments.
    • Enter OVRLP into Notes/Dates and Save All.

Info

The OVRLP flag helps identify overlap courses so we can contact instructors during finals week.

docs.title: 4. Form Handling (Paper)
docs.text:
Staff should:

  • Initial and date the form.
  • Add Student ID and Instructor Email to a tracking list to revisit during finals week.
  • Scan to Laserfiche.

docs.title: 5. Finals Week Procedures
docs.text: AR Techs will email instructors requesting time sheets.

docs.title: 6. Finalizing Records
docs.text:
Once the completed time sheet is received:

  • Scan to Laserfiche.
  • You may accept a scanned/emailed copy of the time sheet.

docs.title: Student Dropped from Class
docs.text: If the student dropped or was dropped from the class, you may accept an email explanation from the instructor in place of the time sheet.

docs.title: Overlapping Class Is a CEC Course
docs.text:
Warning

If the overlapping class involves a CEC (Continuing Education Center) course:

  1. Have the student drop the CEC course.
  2. Register them in the college-credit version of the course.
  3. Either have the student re-register for the CEC course, or ask CEC Admissions to re-register them.

docs.title: Before You Start
docs.text:

  • Email is not entered for CJ and Fire applications.

docs.title: Step 1 — Name Search (Prevent Duplicates)
docs.text:
Before creating a new record, thoroughly search Colleague to ensure no existing record exists.

Search Method Format Partial name + DOB First 2 letters of Last name, first 2 letters of first name, ;bd — e.g., do, jo ;bd 11/20/18 Full name Last name, First name Date of Birth ;bd mm/dd/yy SSN 123456789 Alternate ID (GLINK) ;alt 123456789 CCCID Enter CCCID Person Search Use Colleague Person Search Advanced Search Use Alternate ID (uppercase letters)

docs.text:
Drill into Academic Program on ASUM.

Enter the following information:

  • Name (LFM format)
  • Address / City / State / Zip / County

  • SSN
  • Sex
  • Birth Date
  • Ethnic / Race

After entering demographics, drill into "Additional Info" → select BIO.

docs.title: Step 5 — FINF (Foreign Person Information)
docs.text:
Corresponds to question #12 on the application:

Field Options Alien Status N = Not an Alien (U.S. Citizen) / Y = Alien (Perm. Res or other) Reg # Enter Alien number (A#) for Permanent Resident Card holders Citizenship Country US for United States, or ... to select from list Immigration Status Enter code from question #12: 1 = US Citizen, 2 = Permanent Resident

Info

For Permanent Residents: get a copy of the green card and scan it with the application. The citizenship country is on the Green Card.

For students changing status from F-1 to Permanent Resident/US Citizen: require a new application with International Student Office clearance plus proof of green card/citizenship certificate.

Status Who AB540 (Resident) Fill out AB540 Application Exception (Resident) Military — see Residency Overview pp. 21–22 Foreign Country Resident F-1 International Students / Undocumented Students Out of State Resident No CA residence for more than one year preceding determination date (Ed. Code § 68018) — Question #22 is Yes California Resident Physically present in CA more than one year + one day and demonstrates intent to make CA permanent home (Ed. Code §§ 68017, 68062)

Permanent Resident Green Card Holders

Require students to prove residency if the green card issued date and arrival date are less than 2 years apart.

docs.title: Student Type (Question #19)
docs.text: Code Who CAPL K-8 (pays all fees) CAPU 9–12 (no tuition fee but pays health fee and misc.) CUNRW Entered in STPE for Non-Resident CAP — exempts from out-of-state fees MCHS Middle College High School (no fees) DUALU Dual High School student (no fees) RGLR Everyone else

docs.title: Fraud
docs.text:
If a student reaches out to push through their application:

  1. Have them submit:

    • A photo of a photo ID
    • A selfie holding their photo ID next to their face

  2. If it looks legitimate:

    • Find the student's application confirmation number in the Report Repository.
    • Mark as valid in CCCApply Administrator.
    • Go to XSGRT in Colleague → enter the confirmation number → enter Y → Save All to push into the next processing batch.

  3. If it looks fake:

    • Stop communication.
    • Save a copy of the student's application and documentation to: M Drive > Potential Fraud Applications > Last Name, First Name NO ID Term

docs.title: Determining the Good ID
docs.text:
Priority order:

  1. Financial Aid Record — If one ID has an active or historical Financial Aid record, that is the Good ID.
  2. Record Activity — If neither has Financial Aid, select the ID with the most activity (transcripts, registration history, forms processed, etc.).
  3. Current Enrollment — If both are similar, select the ID associated with current course registrations.

docs.title: For Specialists
docs.text:
1. Submit via email to Jay, Roxana, or Thien.
  2. Include: Student ID numbers for both accounts.

docs.title: If Request Comes from a Student
docs.text:
1. Ask for a photo ID to confirm identity.
  2. Forward the request and ID to Jay, Roxana, or Thien.

After receiving the request, Jay, Roxana, or Thien will route the merge to the appropriate departments.

docs.title: Open Section Enrollment — Soft Student Onboarding
docs.text:
Provide each student submitting a Special Admit Form / Add Card with the handout on how to create an account on sac.dualenroll.com and how to register/drop classes.

  • Student must have an active application (SSO login required).
  • Students will create their on-time profile (including parent approval).
  • Each registration request routes first to the HS counselor for approval, before being submitted to Colleague.
    • Remind students to be in touch with their HS counselor and watch for the approval request email.

docs.title: Home-School Students
docs.text:
Students selecting "Home School" as their high school will be:

  • Prompted to enter a School Approver — with an option to select "Parent is Home School Approver".
    • A link is sent to the home school approver to upload the homeschool affidavit.
  • System creates a step: Review Homeschool Affidavit (routed to A&R).
    • A&R staff review and approve the affidavit before registration activity routes forward.
    • Homeschool Affidavit is saved on the student profile under "Documents" tab.

docs.title: A&R Error Resolution
docs.text:
A&R Staff selects "Other HS not listed" and/or "Homeschool" in the Registration Tab.

The following errors (marked in yellow) are resolved by A&R staff:

Error Text Resolution Course Filled. Either add to wait list or look for an available section. Send back to student to select different section or request waitlisting Section XXX is "Waitlist Full" (20/20). Send back to student to select different section Registration not allowed in CC courses without an academic program. Check Academic Program Status (ASUM/SPRO) The registration period has ended. An add code is required during the "Add" period. Send back to student with Add Auth info This Section was cancelled on XXX. Send back to student to select different section You can't place XXX on the schedule at this time Check section dates — send back to student with info This course is a repeat and does not meet the allowed grades requirement. Check for course repetition and update student Section XXX is "Closed" (40/40). Send back to student to select different section The following required prerequisite for course XXX is not started. Inform student of missing prereq; advise to contact SAC Assessment Center for DualEnrollment counselor appointment. Unofficial transcript required. This course is reserved for students at [specific high school]. Send back to student to select different section. Provide explanation. The following required requisite for course XXX is not fully met. Inform student of missing prereq; advise DualEnrollment counselor appointment. Unofficial transcript required. You have not met the 3.0 GPA requirement to enroll in an honors course. Send back to student to select different section Student does not have a student number Check student has valid (MS) CC Application processed PERSON.ST record with ID 'XXXXXXX' is locked. Cancel out of student file in Colleague, resubmit Error submitting drop: You do not have permission to remove courses during the reg period Check possible reason and update student Could not drop student from course section XXX — student not registered Check STAC if student already dropped; terminate attempt

Info

All other errors are resolved by the DuE Team. Reach out for questions.

docs.title: How to Process Errors
docs.text:
1. Click on the error message (highlighted in yellow).
  2. Take corrective action in Colleague if applicable.
  3. Select the relevant action in dualenroll.com.
  4. Add comments (mandatory when returning to student, HS counselor, or terminating).
  5. Click COMPLETE STEP.

docs.title: Processing Special Admit Forms & Add Cards
Single Enrollments
docs.text:
1. Upload documents to Laserfiche — Add Card and/or Special Admit Form under the appropriate term folder.

  2. If student had a Special Admit Form, flag in COAF:

    • Open COAF in Colleague → search for student.
    • Under Member Of, enter 1CAPF → press Enter.
    • Set Start Date and End Date to match the semester term dates (use term abbreviation to auto-populate, e.g., 2025SP).
    • Save and exit.

  3. Enter waiver in STPE — check start dates and home location of the course.

  4. Register the student into the requested section.

    • If full: do not register — let student know to register in another section or waitlist.

docs.title: Batch Enrollments
docs.text:
1. Download documents to M Drive — batch enrollment Add Cards and Special Admit Forms for the term.

  2. Batch-flag Special Admit Form students in COAF:

    • SLED → load Student IDs with Special Admit Forms.
    • AASM → apply 1CAPF flag for the appropriate semester.
    • Save and exit.

  3. Batch Registration:

    • Load Student IDs in SLED.
    • Load section in SCBL.
    • Batch-register under ARSTAFF reg group in Non-Update mode to check errors.
    • Remove students with non-reg-restriction errors from SLED.
    • Run Non-Update mode again to double-check.
    • Run final batch-register with bypass.

docs.title: Registration into Closed Sections During Add Period
docs.text:
Staff may override the registration restriction for closed dual enrollment sections designated for specific high schools only when all of the following criteria are met:

Criterion Requirement ✅ Dual Enrollment Office Approval All DE Office registration requests for the section have been fully processed ✅ Course Modality Course must be fully online (not hybrid or in-person) ✅ Student Eligibility Student is from another high school or is home-schooled ✅ Timing & Instructor Approval Request is during the add period and instructor add authorization is on file

If all criteria are met → proceed to register. If any criterion is not met → do not override. Refer to the Dual Enrollment Office.

docs.text:
See the Applications — Duplicate ID Merge Requests section for the full procedure.

For full merge processing steps (after the request is submitted), see the AR Tech Duplicate ID page.

Summary of priorities for Good ID: 1. Financial Aid Record (always wins) 2. Most record activity 3. Current enrollment

docs.title: Students Arriving Without a Complete Application
docs.text:
No SAC Application on File: 1. Student must create a CCC ID on CCCApply. 2. Provide a paper application. 3. Admissions staff will manually enter the application. 4. Student can register within an hour once processed.

Student Claims They Applied but No Record Is Found: - Option 1: Ask student to log into CCCApply and provide their confirmation number and CCCApply ID → load a paper application. - Option 2: If student cannot log in or retrieve details → provide a paper application and follow "No SAC Application on File" steps.

Student Has a Spring Application but No Summer/Fall Application: - Provide a paper application (they should already have a CCC ID). - Load the paper application.

docs.title: Updating Student Residency
docs.text:
AB 540 Students: - Students should submit the AB 540 Dynamic Form. - If no SSO access: accept a paper AB 540 form. - Admissions staff manually updates residency status.

Non-Resident Students: - Option 1 (documentation): - ID: California ID or school ID (Aeries screenshot with student photo). - Transcripts: HS transcript showing courses at a CA high school for the past 2 years. - Citizenship: HS Demographic Report showing U.S. citizenship, or HS transcript showing U.S. as birthplace. - Option 2 (no documentation available): - Student must submit a Reclass Form (via Dynamic Form or paper).

docs.text:
Home-school students enrolling through Dual Enrollment are handled through sac.dualenroll.com. See the Dual Enrollment — Home-School Students section for the full procedure.

docs.title: Rule
docs.text:
Students must have two years of the same foreign language with a grade of C- or higher in the last semester.

  • Must be the same language (e.g., two years of French — not one year of Spanish and one year of French).

docs.title: ❌ Does Not Meet Proficiency (last semester grade too low)

docs.text:
Course Term Grade French I FA17 B French I SP18 C Spanish I FA18 C Spanish I SP19 B

(Different languages — does not qualify)

docs.text:
To enter Language Proficiency, go to EXTS and follow the steps below.

docs.title: SCC Special Admit Forms
docs.text: A&R staff may process Santiago Canyon College (SCC) Special Admit forms received in our office.

docs.text:
1. Accept and review the form received in our office.

Reciprocal Processing

docs.title: Subpoenas
docs.text:
Needs to Be Reviewed for Updates

This procedure may need to be reviewed for any policy updates.

docs.title: 1. Notice to Student
docs.text:
• If a Release of Authorization form is attached to the subpoena, no mailed notice is required.
  • If no release is attached:
    1. Prepare the Letter and Notice to Consumer or Employee.
    2. Place the notice in an envelope and mail to the student using:
      • Certified Mail
      • Return Receipt Requested
    3. Deliver the prepared envelope and certified mail forms to Bee in the Mail Room.
    4. Upon receipt:
      • Attach the returned receipt to the filed subpoena.
      • If the mail is returned, attach the returned mail to the filed subpoena as well.
  • Wait 7 calendar days for a response.
  • If no response is received after 7 days, begin processing the subpoena.

docs.title: 2. Review and Distribution
docs.text:
1. Review the subpoena to determine what records are being requested.
  2. Forward the subpoena to all applicable departments to gather the required information.
  3. Compile all received documentation.
  4. Send completed records and documents to Risk Management.

docs.title: A. Application Records
docs.text:
• LaserFiche — Print the student's application.
  • OpenCCCApply Report Center — Print the student's application.

docs.title: B. Transcripts (TRAN)
docs.text:
1. Enter the Student ID.
  2. Save.
  3. P → FormFusion
  4. Use the printing code.
  5. Save and update.

docs.title: C. Other Student Records (If Requested)
docs.text: Copy all relevant correspondence and student records including instructor rosters, grades, and programs. Ensure all documents are in order and legible. Only include the requested student's records. Redact or white out other students' information as needed.

docs.title: D. Full Grades and Rosters (If Required)
docs.text:
1. STAC — Print course list.
  2. M: Drive → Instructor Grades:
    • Search by semester.
    • Search by instructor and section.
    • Locate rosters for each registered class.
  3. Redact or black out all other students' information.
  4. Convert all documents to PDF and ensure redactions are applied correctly.

docs.title: 4. Subpoenas Requiring Financial Information
docs.text:
Contact the appropriate offices for financial records:

  • CO/Auxiliary Services — Jennie Adams, Ext. 46433
  • Financial Aid — Thuy Huong Nguyen, Ext. 46485

Email the department(s) with the subpoena request and student name/ID. Request that documentation be sent via interoffice mail if applicable.

docs.title: 5. Subpoena Fees Form
docs.text:
• Track all time spent processing the subpoena for billing purposes.
  • Copy the tracked time from Excel and paste it as an image into a letterhead document.
  • Save the file as Subpoena Fees.
  • Convert the document to PDF by right-clicking on the file.

docs.title: 6. Custodian of Records Form
docs.text:
• Complete the Custodian of Records form provided by HR.
  • Attach the completed form to the subpoena package.

docs.title: Document Order
docs.text:
1. Custodian of Records Form
  2. Subpoena Fees Form
  3. Application
  4. Transcripts
  5. Additional subpoena-requested documents

docs.title: Distribution and Filing
docs.text:
• Make copies of all documents.
  • Email the completed subpoena package to Annina Brown.
  • File physical copies in the first drawer located by Mark's office.
  • Include the notification letter to the student in the file.
    • If the letter is returned, file it with the subpoena documentation.

docs.title: 8. Special Notes and Exceptions
docs.text:
Public Defender Subpoenas

Do not pay the $15 fee.

Workers' Compensation Subpoenas

  • Include application and transcripts only.
  • Send documents to the District.

docs.title: Academic Renewal Petitions
docs.text: Reference: Credit Course Repetition Guidelines (PDF)

docs.title: Petition for Academic Renewal WITH Course Repetition
docs.text: Ed Code Section 55042

docs.title: Eligibility
docs.text:
Can only be processed if the C-ID matches for both schools OR approved by the department Dean/Chair. Research must be done by ARII, not the student.

  • Search for C-ID here: c-id.net/courses/search
  • If C-ID is not available, search for the course descriptions from the catalog year the course was taken. Send the petition, transcripts, and course descriptions to the department for review. Search college catalogs at cso.collegesource.com

docs.title: STAC Steps
docs.text:
1. Detail into the course by clicking the icon next to the course number.

    Screenshot Placeholder

    STAC course detail screenshot is in the original Word manual.

  2. Select STRP from the detail screen.

    Warning

    Check if there are other courses marked as "R — Replaced by Colleague". The maximum allowable is 2 R's. On repetition to alleviate substandard grades, only the first two substandard grades may be excluded from GPA calculation (55042).

    Screenshot Placeholder

    STRP screen screenshot is in the original Word manual.

  3. Cancel out, then detail back into the course and select SACD.

    • Credit Type: AR
    • Important: Do not touch the grade or any other fields on this screen.
    • Drill into Note/Comments.

  4. In Notes/Comments:

    • Notes: Enter TRR
    • Comments: Enter the following (with timestamp):

      This section was repeated at "[NAME OF COLLEGE]" with grade "[GRADE]" in "[TERM]" for "[UNITS]"

      Example: This section was repeated at Golden West College with grade A in Spring 2022 for 3 units.

    • Printed Comments:

      Repeated at "[Name of College]"

      Example: Repeated at Golden West College

    Danger

    Printed Comments will appear on the transcript — verify spelling before saving.

    Screenshot Placeholder

    Notes/Comments and Printed Comments screenshots are in the original Word manual.

  5. Go to TRAN to verify the changes appear correctly on the transcript.

    Screenshot Placeholder

    TRAN verification screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Specific Case
docs.text: A student took a course three times with substandard grades, then retook it a fourth time outside the district and passed. Only two of the three previous attempts can be alleviated — the third grade remains on the GPA. In this case, deny the petition and encourage the student to meet with their counselor to discuss an RWO.

docs.title: Petition for Academic Renewal WITHOUT Course Repetition (RWO)
docs.text: Ed Code Section 55046

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Make sure incoming transcripts are evaluated by ARIII before proceeding.
  • For units completed, use Completed units — P grades count.
  • GPA calculation is completed by ARII before processing:
    • Start counting after the last requested substandard grade's term.
    • Count all grades (including D's, excluding F's) to determine GPA.
    • Exclude F's since these are not completed units — the policy counts completed units only.

docs.title: Degrees/Certificates on File
docs.text:
If a degree or certificate appears on the transcript, an RWO can still be processed if the course was not used for degree/certificate requirements. Graduation Specialists verify:

  • The course was not used to fulfill a GE area
  • The course was not used to fulfill the 60-degree unit requirement
  • The course was not used to fulfill the residency requirement
Alpha Range Contact A–G Christine H–O Jessica P–Z Tina

docs.title: STAC / SACD Steps
docs.text:
1. Look for the course listed on the form for the correct term. Detail into SACD for that course.

    Danger

    Verify the course number, term, and grade match between STAC and the petition form before proceeding. If they do not match, the form must be corrected by the student or counselor and initialed.

  2. SACD Entry:

    • Change Credit Type to MR (Manual Repeat)
    • Do not change or delete the grade.
    • Do not change any other fields.
    • Click OK

  3. Run RACT on the Student ID with Academic Level as CC.

docs.title: After Processing
docs.text:
• Run the Transcript (TRAN) to confirm the RWO appears correctly.
  • If "RWO" does not show on the transcript:
    • Double-check that MR was entered in the Credit Type field in SACD.
    • Give to Tech to investigate further.

docs.title: Specific Cases
docs.text:
• Student is currently enrolled in a course for the third time and seeking an RWO for a previous F: Yes, you can process the RWO — their current grade will stand.
  • Can a student request an RWO for the same class a second time? Yes, as long as they meet all criteria on the back of the form (Hung discussed this with Mark 05/13/24).
