docs.title: Residency Notes
docs.text:
Detailed edge cases and special scenarios for residency determination.

Reference documents:

  • CA Residency Overview 2025 (CCCCO)
  • RSCCD Administrative Regulation AR 5015
  • CA Residency Resource Tool (SAC)

docs.title: ScribeHow Guide
docs.text: How to process the Residency Form (ScribeHow)

docs.title: Core Rules
docs.text:
• ✅ Residency can be updated only for the current term.
  • ❌ No retroactive reclassification for a completed term.
  • ✅ Backdating is allowed only if the term has not ended.
  • ⛔ No non-resident tuition refunds after first census (AR 5015).

docs.title: Timing & Backdating
docs.text:
Residency date = one day before term start (e.g., term starts 6/16/25 → enter 6/15/25).

Can Reclassify Cannot Reclassify Term is still in progress Term has ended AND no petition was submitted during that term Petition was submitted before the term ended

Transitional Exception: Applications from 2024SU–2025 may be processed within one year. Starting with 2026 applications, residency must be fixed within the term.

docs.title: Proof Requirements
docs.text:
• 2 proofs required, both dated ≥ one year + one day before term start.
  • If deriving from parent/guardian: 2 proofs from parent/guardian; one must be a CA 540 listing student as dependent.

Common proofs: CA Driver License (issue date), lease/rental agreement, CA voter registration (check voting history dates), CA professional license (DCA search).

Tip

If documentation is incomplete, do not deny immediately — review for alternatives first.

docs.title: Who Is Eligible
docs.text: Eligible Not Eligible U.S. Citizen F-1 students Permanent Resident (incl. conditional) Undocumented students PR applicants (I-485) B visa students Asylees / asylum applicants TD/TN visa holders (unless taking steps toward PR) Refugees / refugee applicants Temporary Protected Status (TPS) DACA (C33 only)

docs.title: Residency Questionnaire
docs.text:
• First-time applicant → NOT required.
  • Returning student → REQUIRED.
  • Students may submit 2 from Group A or 2 from Group B.

docs.title: ASUM
docs.text: • Academic Program → Res Stat: R — California Resident

docs.title: ASPR
docs.text:
• Insert R — California Resident
  • Date = one day before term start
  • Ensure Resident status appears on top

docs.title: Comments (UTEC)
docs.text: Include: Timestamp · "California Residency" · Documents reviewed

docs.title: Final Checklist
docs.text:
• ✅ Save & update records / Save all changes
  • 📞 Call Cashier's Office for rebill (provide Student ID and term)
  • ✅ Verify form is signed & complete

docs.title: AB 540 — Student Forgot to Turn In Form
docs.text:
• Have them fill out the AB 540 form and backdate to the beginning of that term.
  • AB 540 only needs to be submitted once. If previously AB 540, broke enrollment, and submitted new application as Non-Resident, we can backdate their AB 540 status to the beginning of the new term.

docs.title: AB 540 — Switching to CA Resident
docs.text: • If US Citizen: only need naturalization paperwork (for continuing students) and a new application.

docs.title: AB 540 — Homeschooled
docs.text: • Put "homeschooled" in high school information area and ask for high school diploma.

docs.title: AB 540 — Lists CEC
docs.text: • AB 540 is an affidavit — if they attest it is true, process it. Only ask for verification if there is conflicting information between ASUM and the form (per Pilar).

docs.title: AB 540 — Not Living in CA
docs.text:
Danger

Students must be living in CA to qualify. Long-distance learners residing out of state do not qualify per Chancellor's Office (CCCCO Residency Overview, 2025).

docs.title: Active Military
docs.text:
• If stationed in CA: use orders to change status to E or EB. No minimum duration required (per Residency Overview).
    • If home of record is also CA: can make them a CA Resident.
  • Dependents: Spouse/child of active-duty member stationed in CA qualifies for resident classification with no limit on duration. Orders must be ≥ 1 year + 1 day before term start. Only need DEERS card (no copy) and stationed-in-CA orders.
  • If stationed outside CA: provide documentation (MOL or military record) that home of record is CA → update to R. If home of record is a state we can't serve, they cannot take courses with us.

docs.title: Automatic CA Residency (by Ed Code)
docs.text: Category Ed Code Nonresident minor whose parent left California ECS 68070 Self-Supporting Student ECS 68071 Under 2-year care/control of a CA-resident adult ECS 68073 Adult dependent child of CA resident ECS 68076 Graduate of Bureau of Indian Affairs school ECS 68077 Employees of public schools ECS 68078 State government fellowship student (state/CSU) ECS 68081 Native American at Bureau of Indian Affairs school in district ECS 68082 Team USA student athlete training in state ECS 68083

docs.title: B Students
docs.text:
Danger

B visa students are not allowed to take classes with us.

docs.title: C Students (Asylum)
docs.text:
• C08 = asylum applicants.
  • Before 1 year since CA arrival: exempt from non-resident tuition.
  • After 1 year: must show receipt received dates ≥ 1 year + 1 day before semester start, plus 2 proofs of CA Residency.

docs.title: CER (Continuing Education Residents)
docs.text:
1. Check ASUM for the semester applied — if Resident, update on ASPR. No comments needed.
  2. Update home location.
  3. If Financial Aid sends an AIDE error: check dates on ASPR — insert a new R/N line more recent than the CER status if needed.

docs.title: Criminal Justice Academies Students
docs.text: Per Ed Code §76140: a community college may classify a nonresident student hired by a public agency as a resident while enrolled in a police academy training course.

docs.title: DACA
docs.text:
• EAD must be Category C33.
  • Must meet the one year + one day requirement or have a Notice of Action receipt that meets it.
  • No residency questionnaire required — just proofs.

docs.title: Employment Authorization
docs.text:
• If issue date is sufficient: eligible for CA Resident status (EAs are only issued to PR or Asylum applicants per Residency Overview 2023).
  • If not old enough: ask for Notice of Action for the employment authorization.

docs.title: F1 Students Changing to CA Residency
docs.text:
Required: Application for PR or USC status + PR card or Passport + intent to stay in CA + 2 proofs of 1 year + 1 day CA residency.

  1. Fill out a new application (no reclass form needed).
  2. Send to International Students to sign off.
  3. Update ASUM, BIO, ASPR.
  4. Call Cashier's for rebill if needed.

Blended Staff/Student

Send to Job Placement to update demographics from F-1 to Permanent Resident first, then return to process reclass.

Danger

F1 and B1 students do NOT qualify for AB 540.

Note: PR/USC application starts the clock from application date. F1 status does not need to be expired. Does not have to be approved (CACCRAO 4/29/25). Changing F1 → H1: H1 must be approved (Residency Overview p.11).

docs.title: Financial Aid
docs.text:
• CA Residents qualify for more aid than AB 540 students. AB 540 US citizens still receive Pell Grants and fee waivers.
  • If Financial Aid asks to update the Student Type date: do not update. They likely mean the residency status date — check ASPR.

docs.title: Foster Youth
docs.text:
• Will be flagged as non-residents. If everything else checks out, change to CA Resident and note: student is foster youth / guardian is other than parent.
  • Refer to Guardian Scholars: (714) 564-6302.

docs.title: H1 Students
docs.text: • Need H1 visa info showing ≥ 1 year + 1 day issue date and 2 proofs of CA residency.

docs.title: H4 Students
docs.text: • Need spouse's/parent's H1 visa + H4 visa + student's 2 proofs of CA residency.

docs.title: High School Students
docs.text: STPE → correct semester → Overload Petition Status → NONRE Non-Resident Waiver → Save and Update → Rebill.

docs.title: If Student Is Already CA Resident but Changing Citizenship Status
docs.text: (e.g., PR or DACA → US Citizen) - No new proof of residency needed. Just new citizenship documentation and a new application. - US Citizenship Naturalization supersedes all dates — expired PR card doesn't matter once naturalized.

docs.title: Incoming Freshmen
docs.text: • If they marked something wrong: provide high school transcripts showing they've been in CA for at least the past year.

docs.title: K1 Visa
docs.text: • As long as issue date is ≥ 1 year + 1 day, can be used as citizenship status.

docs.title: Out of Country School
docs.text: • May use student visa in passport to prove they were abroad specifically to study. Cannot establish residency in another country on a student visa.

docs.title: Out of State School
docs.text:
• Use billing statement showing out-of-state tuition as one proof. Does not work for private colleges.
  • For private or online schools: just need CA ID ≥ 1 year + 1 day and other proof.

docs.title: Out of State Taxes
docs.text: • If student filed taxes out of state in past 2 years: need their most recent CA 540. Use CA ID ≥ 1 year + 1 day + CA 540.

docs.title: Permanent Residents
docs.text:
• Can use US birth certificate, naturalization papers, or passport for citizenship.
  • I-797 (Notice of Action) received date can be used to backdate presence.
  • Application for extension or renewal can be used — does not have to be approved.
  • If applying for US Citizenship: ask for N-400 if PR Card is expired (shows 24-month extension).

docs.title: R1/R2 Visas
docs.text: • OK to establish CA Residency (per Pilar).

docs.title: Refugees
docs.text:
• Immediate exemption from non-resident tuition within first year (Ed Code 68075.6, effective Jan 1, 2018).
  • After first year: must provide 2 proofs of CA residency.

docs.title: State of Last High School Not CA and Student Is Under 19
docs.text: • Contract with district + high school transcripts required.

docs.title: Student Error
docs.text:
• Filed taxes outside CA for a recent year → must provide evidence they filed in CA.
  • Put wrong country → fix it and make notes in ASPR.

docs.title: Two Years Care and Control Proof for Guardian Required
docs.text:
• Can change residency back and make notes in ASPR (per Pilar), as long as everything else checks out.
  • Make sure the student is a US Citizen.

docs.title: Unable to Establish CA Residency
docs.text:
• TD/TN visa holders: ineligible unless taking steps toward PR or out-of-status (expired visa).
  • Financially dependent on out-of-state/out-of-country parents (more than $750/year).

docs.title: Unhoused Youth
docs.text:
• Homeless youth under 25 will be flagged as non-residents. Ask for usual documentation; note "student is homeless youth under 25" in ASPR. Leave permanent address blank.
  • Homeless students: ask for CA ID or license + one form of documentation.
  • See if they qualify for AB 540 as well.
  • Refer to Robert Manson: (714) 564-6247.

docs.title: Unhoused Students
docs.text: • Accept a letter from their shelter as one proof of residency.

docs.title: Veterans
docs.text:
• DD-214 shows separation within last 3 years → mark as Exempt (E in ASPR).
  • Valid CA Driver License + home of record is CA on DD-214 → mark as Resident (per Pilar).
  • Stationed in CA > 1 year immediately before discharge → exempt from non-resident tuition for up to 1 year if affidavit of intent to establish CA residency is filed. Must be used within 2 years of discharge.
  • If VRC sends signed VACA form → update ASPR to VACA (per Pilar).

docs.title: Voted Outside of CA
docs.text:
• Does not need voter registration to disprove.
  • Ask for regular 2 residency documents and update (per Pilar).

docs.title: General Additional Notes
docs.text:
• If student mentions B visa: need proof they are out of status (B visa = vacation visa, no school allowed).
  • If we have the student's approval notice, the physical green card is not required.
  • Port of entry documentation: if we can connect the dots they've been in CA and the date of establishing CA residency is within ~6 months of entry, they may have a case.
  • If flagged for taxes or claimed by out-of-state parents: need CA 540 as financial evidence.
  • If approved for a previous semester but marked non-resident on a new application: can use previous semester's documents.
  • Always need a new application if changing residency or status for older semesters (not required for the current semester).
  • Always need a reclass form unless it is the student's first semester with us.
  • Only need one reclass/AB 540/application if processing for multiple consecutive semesters — e.g., processing Fall 2019 also covers Spring 2020.
  • If flagged as Non-Resident for an SCC application: send to SCC to fix it — we don't have access to their application.
  • Students only need to provide documentation for the specific semester — just update on ASPR.

docs.title: SB554 Special Admit Forms (Dynamic Forms)
docs.text: In Spring 2022, the Special Admit Form was updated to include SB554, which applies to adult learners in Continuing Education who have not yet graduated but wish to enroll in college credit courses. These students are similar to Special Admit high school students and require the Special Admit Form to be completed and their approved courses entered into STPE. Course approvals are handled by their Continuing Education (CE) counselor.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Warning

Ensure the student has a College Credit application on file before processing. If no CC application exists, deny and explain that one must be on file to process.

  • SB554 students do not need to submit another form if the courses have already been approved — they can register for a different section directly or ask their counselor for help.
  • SB554 forms must be submitted by CEC counselors per Pilar.

docs.title: SHAP
docs.text:
• Update Student Type from RGLR to SB554
  • Keep Admit Status as Y

Screenshot Placeholder

SHAP entry screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: SPRO
docs.text:
• Update Student Type from RGLR to SB554

Screenshot Placeholder

SPRO entry screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: COAF
docs.text:
1. Enter the Student ID number and press Enter.
  2. Type 1SB54 into an open line and press Enter.
  3. Enter the start and end dates of the semester the student is requesting.
  4. You can type the term code to auto-populate dates (e.g., 2025SI in both Start Date and End Date).

Screenshot Placeholder

COAF entry screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: STPE
docs.text:
• Enter the waiver for the student to register.
  • Enter NONRE for Overload Petition Status.

docs.title: RGN
docs.text:
• Register the student in the requested classes.
  • If the course is full: do not register — notify the student the course was full and that they can register into a different section or waitlist themselves.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Go to the RECEIVE screen in Parchment.
  2. Download the transcripts.

  3. Name each file using this format:

    Lastname, Firstname_STUDENTID_School name

    If no Colleague ID is found:

    Lastname, Firstname_NO ID_School name

  4. Move the downloaded transcripts to the Parchment folder in the M Drive (see Move to M Drive).

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Log into XAP Control Center at: XAP Transcripts Report

  2. Navigate to: eTranscriptCA → Reports → Transcripts we've received → "Detail"

  3. Set "Date From" to the previous day transcripts were downloaded, and "Today's Date" as the end date.

  4. Check the XAP folder 2 "Downloaded Completed" to see which transcripts still need to be downloaded.

  5. Download the received transcripts.

  6. Name each file in this format:

    Lastname, Firstname_STUDENTID_School name

    If no Colleague ID is found:

    Lastname, Firstname_NO ID_School name

  7. Move the downloaded transcripts to the XAP folder in the M Drive (see Move to M Drive).

docs.title: Logging Transcripts and Test Scores in IASU
Steps
docs.text:
1. Go to IASU and search for the institution where the transcript comes from.

  2. If the institution is in IASU: Drill in and log the transcript using the fields below.

  3. If the institution is NOT in IASU: Enter the school's name on a new line, then choose the school code that matches the name and address on the transcript legend.

Screenshot Placeholder

Screenshots of the IASU entry screen are in the original Word manual. Add them here when available.

docs.title: Field Reference
docs.text: Field What to Enter Transcript type Pick the code that applies: College / High School / International transcript / External exams (AP/IB/CLEP Scores) Date received Current date Status RECD — Official Received Year attended Year of first and last term attended Start/End dates See semester date codes below Degree received Enter if Associate's or Bachelor's degree is found on transcript Comment Timestamp + note (use one of the source codes below)

docs.title: Semester Date Codes
docs.text: Semester Start Date End Date Summer 06/01/YY 07/31/YY Fall 08/01/YY 12/31/YY Intersession / Spring 01/01/YY 05/31/YY

docs.title: Comment Source Codes
docs.text:
Use one of these codes in the comment:

  • Parchment
  • XAP
  • adm email
  • counter

docs.title: Degree Entry
docs.text: Drill into the degree box → degree date → type ... (3 dots) → click to choose a degree code → click Open → enter the degree earned date → single Save → Update.

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
Multiple Entries

Make sure to click on 1 and Insert a new line if there are already entries — do not overwrite existing records.

docs.title: District Transcripts with Multiple Institutions
docs.text:
If you receive a transcript from a school district with multiple institutions, add all institutions the student attended to IASU. Refer to the transcript legend for school codes.

Common district transcripts include: - Riverside Community College District - Los Angeles Community College District - San Diego Community College District

docs.title: Duplicate Transcripts
docs.text:
If a transcript was previously submitted:

  1. Check Laserfiche for the original transcript — verify dates attended and units completed.
  2. If no change: Add (Duplicate) to the timestamp comment and at the end of the filename.
  3. Example: Norris, Chuck (Duplicate)_1711846_Fullerton college
  4. If there is a change: Update Start/End dates.

docs.title: Legacy Transcript Colleague Build
docs.text: Use this procedure when a transcript request comes in for a student whose record predates the current Colleague system.

docs.title: Step 1 — Search for Existing Record
docs.text:
1. If no record is found in Colleague, check for an old record in Laserfiche (search by name, DOB, or SSN).
  2. If not found in Laserfiche, check the Black/Blue books in the breakroom.

Vault Records

There are still some records in the vault that have not been scanned yet. For additional support, see Amber Stapleton.

Screenshot Placeholder

Screenshots of sample old transcripts are in the original Word manual. Add them here when available.

docs.title: NAE — Name Search
docs.text:
• Go to NAE and perform a name search.
  • If no record found → Click Add.
  • Add: Name, Social Security Number, Sex, Birthday.

Screenshot Placeholder

NAE entry screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: ASUM / SHAP
docs.text:
• ASUM: Drill into the first line on Acad Program.
  • SHAP: Follow the steps in the original Word manual screenshots.

Screenshot Placeholder

ASUM and SHAP screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: DADD — Alternative IDs
docs.text:
• Add the student's Perm. No. to Alternative IDs.
  • Add 000 or copy the "Student ID" from the Laserfiche metadata.
  • Type: CPERM

docs.title: SPRO — Deactivate Academic Program
docs.text:
Important

Deactivate the Academic Program in SPRO before proceeding.

Screenshot Placeholder

SPRO deactivation screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Step 3 — TRRQ (Transcript Requests)
docs.text:
Info

Skip this step if the old transcript does NOT list a transcript request.

  • If more than two transcript requests are on the old transcript, only enter the first two to show that the student already used their two free transcripts.
Field Value For transcript sent to another school ID/Name: name of school; use 1M or 1C For "Personal" transcripts ID/Name: "Official Transcript" code; Deliv Method: 1X Date Produced / Request Date Date shown on the transcript No of Copies Number of copies requested on the transcript Print Now NO Comments Do NOT timestamp — leave comment: Build Laserfiche record 000(Perm. No.)

Screenshot Placeholder

TRRQ entry examples are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Step 4 — Add Colleague ID to Laserfiche
docs.text:
• When the Colleague profile is complete, add the Colleague ID to the metadata of the old transcript in Laserfiche.
  • If the transcript order came from Parchment, add the Colleague ID as both the Verify ID and Unverified ID.

docs.title: Step 5 — Email AR Technician
docs.text: Email the AR Technician the Built Laserfiche Form along with the Laserfiche transcript so the old transcript can be merged into the student's Colleague record.

docs.title: Important Restriction
docs.text:
Warning

Legacy transcripts can only be mailed or picked up in person by the student. If a request is made for an electronic transcript, cancel the order and leave the following note:

"The student has a record from before 1996. Hard copy transcript only. Please resubmit a mailed transcript request."

docs.title: Move Transcripts to M Drive & Distribute
Location
docs.text:
Go to the SAC — eTrans Download folder in the M Drive.

Directory: SAC -- eTrans Downloads

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Log received transcripts to student records in Colleague via the IASU screen.

  2. See Logging Transcripts in IASU for detailed steps.

    Correction

    Use RECD Official Received instead of PEND Pending.

  3. Move the downloaded transcripts to the 'Parchment' and 'XAP' folders.

  4. Check the 'JST Military Trans' folder for new transcripts — look in the "Import to Laserfiche" sub-folder.

  5. Rename files to the same format as Parchment and XAP, then log into IASU.

  6. Check the admissions@sac.edu folder for new transcripts.

  7. Distribute transcripts from admissions@sac.edu, JST Military Trans, Parchment, and XAP to:

  8. AR III — College and JST transcripts
  9. AR II — High School transcripts

  10. Distribution is by Alpha Breakdown.

  11. Copy & paste transcripts from admissions@sac.edu, Parchment, and XAP folder 1 "Import to Laserfiche" to the 'QF — Import to Laserfiche' folder.

    Warning

    Do NOT put them in the "ARCHIVES" folder. JST Military Trans transcripts do NOT go in the QF folder.

  12. Move transcripts from all source folders (folder 1 "Import to Laserfiche") to folder 2 "Downloaded Completed".

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Log into your Parchment Exchange account at exchange.parchment.com and click Resume Printing.

  2. Process all OPEN ORDERS.

  3. Resolve all PROBLEM ORDERS.

  4. Go to the History folder and enter the DID number to find the order, then print out shipping labels for Overnight Orders with FedEx.

    • Automatic FedEx pick-up days are Wednesday and Thursday — cancel or schedule accordingly.

  5. Go to the STRQ screen in Colleague to print paper transcripts to be mailed out.

  6. Click Pause Printing on Parchment after paper transcripts are printed.

IGETC Certification Check

Check for awarded IGETC certification on the last page of each printed transcript. Print a copy of the IGETC from Laserfiche to be sent with the transcript order.

docs.title: ARIII Procedures
docs.text: ARIII handles academy enrollments, incoming college transcript evaluation, application batch processing, petitions, and more.

docs.title: Responsibilities
docs.text: Area Description CJA Enrollment Batch enrollment for Criminal Justice Academy students Fire Tech Enrollment Fire Tech and Lifeguard ISA enrollment Instructor Change Forms Unit changes, reinstatements, EA drops, No Show drops, grade changes, class transfers Application Batches Downloading and clearing application batch errors in EAPP/AIBD Fraudulent Applications Processing fraud flags and identity verification Petitions EW petitions, N1 drops, Change of Info, Unit Overload, Pass-Alongs, Probation/Dismissal Transcripts — Incoming Colleges Total transfer unit evaluation/processing, GE evaluation, pseudo processing Academic Probation Probation codes, dismissal, and intervention

docs.title: Academic Probation
docs.text: You can view a student's academic standing via STAT → drill down into "Academic Standing."

docs.title: Academic Probation (GPA-based)
docs.text:
Triggered after attempting 12+ units with a cumulative GPA below 2.0

Code Meaning A1 1st semester academic probation A2 2nd semester academic probation AD Academic Dismissal (3rd consecutive semester)

docs.title: Progress Probation (W-based)
docs.text:
Triggered after attempting 12+ units where 50% or more of courses have a "W" grade

Code Meaning P1 1st semester progress probation P2 2nd semester progress probation PD Progress Dismissal (3rd consecutive semester)

docs.title: Combined Codes
docs.text: When a student falls within both academic and progress probation, the codes are joined.

docs.title: Appeal Approved
docs.text: When you see AP at the end of any of the codes above, it means the appeal was approved — either automatically or via a manual update by Admissions (when a student completes and is approved for the CCPG appeal form and meets criteria 1–3).

docs.title: Probation/Dismissal Intervention
docs.text: See Petitions — Probation/Dismissal Intervention.

docs.title: STEP 1 — Download the Information
docs.text:
EAPP (first run — No, Yes, No): 1. Type SAC... and select the date and time for the batch you are scheduled for. 2. Run using No, Yes, No. 3. Make sure the output device is H (Hold the report) — then Save All.

EAPP (second run — No, No, Yes): 1. Once the first run finishes, run EAPP again using No, No, Yes. 2. Save out and exit the report.

Screenshot Placeholders

EAPP run screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: STEP 2 — Clear Person/Address Duplicates
docs.text:
1. Go to AIBD (enter SAC... again to find the batch).
  2. Drill into "Resolve Person/Address Duplicate".

For each error, drill into the line:

Address/Person errors: - If info matches → input Y in the Combine box → Save. - If info does not match → input N in the Combine box → Save.

Application errors (majors/academic programs): - Always input N — do not combine. - Make sure to check for multiple majors — put N for all of them.

  1. Once all ELF errors are reviewed, click Save All.

docs.title: STEP 3 — Run EAPP to Clear Errors
docs.text:
1. Go back to EAPP and run again using No, No, Yes.
  2. Repeat Steps 2 and 3 until drilling into "Resolve Person/Address Duplicate" in AIBD shows: "No records found".

docs.title: STEP 4 — Clear Person Errors (Line 1) and Institution Errors (Line 5)
docs.text:
Once all ELF errors are cleared:

  • MN = personal info errors (should all show Is at this point — if not, repeat steps 2 and 3).
  • IN = institution info errors.

Only drill into INs that have Es (errors).

For Line 1 bio errors: - Drill in, review, and enter Y or N in Combine based on whether it is the same student. - If two different students share the same SSN: 1. Drill into the student on the left → go to NAE → remove the SSN. 2. Mark N (no) to not merge. 3. Single Save twice.

For Line 5 institution errors: - Drill in and always put Y.

docs.title: STEP 5 — Done!
docs.text:
• Rerun EAPP.
  • The batch is complete when all Es are Is.

docs.title: Example 1: Student Has Two Ethnicities
docs.text:
The most common error is when a student has both Hispanic and Non-Hispanic.

  1. Cancel out of Phase 2 Error Messages.
  2. Go into student record → NAE.
  3. Select Line 1 of Ethnic(s).
  4. Delete Line 1 (should be blank) → Single Save until back at AIBD.

docs.title: Example 2: Invalid Student Program (Department)
docs.text:
1. Go to PROG and look up the program.
  2. Go to SPRO and drill into the program → update the department.

docs.title: Example 3: Invalid Student Program (Catalog)
docs.text:
1. Let Techs know the program is no longer valid for the current year.
  2. Delete the student record and hand-load.

docs.title: Example 4: Application Ambiguity Exists
docs.text:
1. Close out of Phase 2 errors and return to the main AIBD screen.
  2. DELETE this application (put Y in the delete column) — write down student info to look up their application on the production site.
  3. Load the application manually.

docs.title: Example 5: Other Error
docs.text: • Delete and load manually.

docs.title: Example 6: Student Applied for a Program They Already Graduated From
docs.text:
1. SPRO → go to program → drill in → add a line for Inactive.
  2. Process the rest of the batch.
  3. Delete the Inactive line.
  4. Add a new liberal arts program for the student instead.

docs.title: CJ/Fire General Information
docs.text:
• For inquiries or issues with sections, email the CJ/Fire contacts directly from your personal account to keep the Academies email uncluttered.

  • Starting Fall 2026, sections that end in a later term will belong to that term.

    • Example: A course that begins in May 2026 and ends in October 2026 is a Fall 2026 class — you can register the student if they have a Fall 2026 application.

  • We allow a letter from a police department for residency for CJ per:

    • CA Education Code § 76140.5
    • "A community college may classify a nonresident student who has been hired by a public agency, as a resident for purposes of enrollment in and completion of police academy training courses at a community college, if the student has passed all other requirements of the public agency and if written assurances are provided by the public agency that it intends to classify the student as a peace officer upon successful completion of the police academy training course."

docs.title: 1. Prepare the File
docs.text:
• Save the Excel file from Teams as a CSV file. Clean it up and upload to the Online Report Repository (AR0160).
  • Naming Convention: Section Course Name and Number, Semester and Year.
    • Example: 37362 CJA 034C 2023FA.csv

docs.title: 2. Match Students
docs.text:
• Copy and paste to a new sheet on the CSV and use the MATCH formula to find existing students: 
    =MATCH(C2,Sheet1!$B$1:$B$50,0)
    • Insert a column → Enter =match( → Click first last name on Sheet 1 → comma → Click Sheet 2 and highlight all last names → F4, comma, zero, close parentheses → Enter → Pull down.
    • Or just copy IDs to each student and fill in what's missing.
  • Check if students are able to register or if they need new applications.
    • If they need a new app: find the application in the packet, load the new application, mark it, and Laserfiche it.

docs.title: 3. Find Missing IDs
docs.text: • For students without a match, search Colleague in NAE to find their Student ID number and add to Sheet 1.

docs.title: 4. Create SLED List
docs.text:
• Once every student has an ID, create a list in SLED (list name: tp) with all Student ID numbers.
  • Save All.

docs.title: 5. Add CJA Section to SCBL
docs.text:
• Add CJA section to SCBL (list name: cjtp).
  • Make sure the CJA course falls within the dates of the term.

docs.title: 6. Batch Enroll in BRGN
docs.text:
Step 1 — Check for errors (no changes made):

Field Value Section Block CJER Reg User Group ARSTAFF Date First day of class listed on the packet Update No Saved list name ER

Step 2 — Enroll the students:

Field Value Section Block CJER Reg User Group ARSTAFF Date First day of class listed on the packet Update Yes Saved list name ER

Warning

SAVE THIS OUTPUT.

docs.title: 7. Save Course Roster via SROS
docs.text:
• Term: Current Term
  • Section number: /xxxxx

Warning

SAVE THIS OUTPUT.

docs.title: 8. Wrap Up
docs.text:
• Log completion date in the Excel file.
  • Delete the name search Excel file in Teams.
  • Scan and save the whole packet (Paper Packet, BRGN Output, SROS Output; combine through Adobe) to: M:\Instructor Grade Book\Term YYYY - Instructor Folders\00CJA
  • Email Martin or Lisa with a list of sections ready for grade (new process: complete the request on Dynamic Forms).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
When completing a new application for a student, go to ASUM and click the paper icon next to an open application field. Input:

  • Start Term
  • Academic Program (from application)
  • Admit Status
  • Residency Status (R)
  • Student Type (RGLR)

docs.title: Year-Long Sections
docs.text:
• Starting 2025SP, year-long sections are built for the term in which the section ends.
    • Example: A class starting in 2025SP but ending in 2026SP is built as a 2026SP course.

docs.title: BIDDLE (FAC 008)
docs.text:
• If we have a Course Repetition Petition OR if the student has not previously taken the class: register the student. Override any restrictions encountered.
  • Save the SROS file.

STAC: 1. Enter FRT in Notes/Dates. 2. Add a comment in Comments. 3. Save All.

Screenshot Placeholder

STAC notes/comments screenshots for BIDDLE are in the original Word manual.

Once registration (and application loading if needed) is complete:

  • Scan and save the whole packet (Paper Packet, BRGN Output, SROS Output, Course Repetition Forms; combine through Adobe) to: M:\Instructor Grade Book\Term YYYY - Instructor Folders\00FTA

docs.title: Lifeguard ISA
docs.text:
• Terry should only submit once, October of the following year.
  • He should check throughout the year for whoever needs applications, CAP forms, non-resident fixes, etc.
  • We would need to edit the Name Search file to narrow down who we can register.

docs.title: 1. Access CAP Forms
docs.text:
• Receive enrollment request from coordinator.
  • Access the OneDrive link to retrieve CAP forms for the section.
  • Verify all necessary documentation is available.

docs.title: 2. Batch Flag Students
docs.text: • Batch flag all students in the system to enable the Name Search to identify if they have already submitted applications.

docs.title: 3. Run Name Search
docs.text: The Name Search returns: - Student ID numbers - Last enrolled term - Last term applied - Student type (Regular or CAPU) - CAPU form on file status (Yes/No)

docs.title: 4. Non-Update Batch Registration
docs.text: • Run a non-update batch registration to identify which students are eligible — this test run shows feasibility without making changes.

docs.title: 5. Cross-Reference with Master Sheet
docs.text:
• Match batch registration results with students from the master sheet.
  • Highlight students who need to be registered and are eligible.

docs.title: 6. Filter CAPU Students
docs.text: • Filter to show only CAPU students, then filter further to show only those with a CAPU form on file.

docs.title: 7. Verify Eligibility
docs.text:
• Check last term attended and last term applied for each CAPU student.
  • Confirm eligibility.
  • Highlight eligible students.

docs.title: 8. Register Students
docs.text:
• Filter to show only highlighted students.
  • Process batch registration for all highlighted students.

docs.title: 9. Return Unregistered Students
docs.text:
• Compile list of unhighlighted (unregistered) students.
  • Email the list to the coordinator (e.g., Terry).

docs.title: Coordinator Process for Next Enrollment Round
docs.text:
1. Receive unregistered student list from specialist.
  2. Do not upload the standard blue and white Microsoft Form sheet.
  3. Use the running list of unregistered students provided.
  4. Add new students only from the blue and white Microsoft Form Excel (anyone who signed up after the previous submission).
  5. Upload the combined running list (previous unregistered + new students) for the next enrollment request.

docs.title: Fraudulent Applications
docs.text:
1. Get the student's photo ID. Once identity is verified:

    • Go to Report Repository and find the student's application confirmation number.
    • Input the confirmation number into XSGRT:
      • Use the confirmation ID (from CCC Apply) as the Application ID — it will populate XSGRT with student information.
      • Enter Y in the Reset field.
      • Click Save All.

  2. Upload the student ID to Laserfiche.

  3. The student's application will be downloaded in the next batch.

  4. After the application is downloaded:

    • Name search to find the ID.
    • Place a PERC hold (1PF) and add an end date with a note: "Reviewed their ID to verify identity."

Info

If both SAC and SCC have holds, we can clear both holds if the student presents documentation to us.

docs.title: Prior to Grades Assigned
docs.text:
RGN: 1. Enter Student ID. 2. Find the course. 3. Change the units. 4. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical card.

Note

No need to request a rebill — bill will run automatically overnight.

Screenshot Placeholder

RGN unit change screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: After Grades Assigned
docs.text:
STAC: 1. Enter Student ID. 2. Detail into the section. 3. Update units. 4. Click Save All. 5. Reach out to Cashier's for rebill.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical card.

Screenshot Placeholder

STAC unit change screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Reinstatement
docs.text:
RGCS: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Copy or write down the original drop date. 3. Enter the following fields:

Field Value Status A (Active) Date Original drop date (if weekend, use Monday) Status Reason RE Drop Grade Delete the W or any grade Last Attended Date Delete anything in this field Never Attended Delete anything in this field
  1. Click Save All.

STAC: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Go to Line 2 of Status/Dt/Time/Reason to see the drop time. 3. Go back to Line 1 and update the add time to at least one minute after the drop time. 4. Delete grade if any. 5. Click Save All.

RGN: Verify the student is now registered in the class.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical card.

Screenshot Placeholders

RGCS and STAC reinstatement screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Excessive Absence Drops
docs.text:
When EA Drops Occur

EA Drops occur between the Census date and the last day to drop with a "W".

For faculty requesting late EA drops: email them that the deadline has passed, inform them we cannot accept or backdate such drops, and remind them that per the catalog, dropping is ultimately the student's responsibility. Faculty must get Division Dean permission before requesting a late EA or Drop.

RGN: Enter Student ID and detail into the course.

SRGC: 1. Enter the following fields:

Field Value Status D (Dropped) — date defaults to today Status Reason EA (Excessive Absence) Drop Grade W Last Attended Date Request Date of the form (if already filled, leave alone)
  1. Click Save All.
    • If the course is tied to a higher-level course or has a corequisite:
      • Check XSRWS for a waiver. If none, drop the higher-level/corequisite course as well using reason PD.

STAC: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. With an EA drop, sometimes an empty drop line is added. Click on a Status/Dt/Time/Reason field. 3. Click the ">" arrow to check through multiple lines for empty drop lines. 4. Click Delete to remove any empty drop lines. 5. Update Grade to W. 6. Add Comments with timestamp. 7. Click Save All.

Possible Error — Class is active in STAC but does not show on RGN: 1. Check in STAC if the program is CC or CE. 2. If CC, check RGCS to see if a grade was already entered in error.

Screenshot Placeholders

RGN, SRGC, and STAC EA drop screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: On Time No Show Drops
docs.text:
Verify before processing: - Card was submitted by instructor (ideally from instructor or division email) or on Dynamic Forms. - Was submitted prior to class drop deadline (check SRGD). - Form is complete (including section number).

NS vs N1

  • NS = No Show submitted late by faculty.
  • N1 = Special circumstances (VP of Academic Affairs, Dean, student, A&G, etc.).
  • NS is considered late if submitted on or after census. Late NS requires Division Dean and VP signature (auto-routed with Dynamic Forms).

RGSD: Backdate to first day of class. (If student added after first day, backdate to the day the student added the course.)

RGN: Enter Student ID and detail into the course.

SRGC:

Field Value Status D (Dropped) — date defaults to RGSD date Status Reason NS Drop Grade Delete anything Last Attended Date Delete anything Never Attended Y

Click Save All. (Drop corequisite/higher-level course with PD if applicable.)

STAC: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Remove any empty drop lines using the ">" arrow and Delete. 3. Go to Line 2 to see add time. 4. Update drop time on Line 1 to at least one minute after add time. 5. Add Comments with timestamp. 6. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical form.

docs.title: If Student Dropped Before You Can Process
docs.text:
STAC: 1. Update Drop date to first day of class. 2. Update Reason to NS. 3. Remove W or any grade. 4. Click Save All.

RGCS: 1. Update Status Reason to NS if not already. 2. Remove W or grade. 3. Update Never Attended to Y. 4. Click Save All.

docs.title: Late NS/N1 Drops When Grade Is on File
docs.text:
STAC (skip if grade has not yet been assigned): 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Delete grade if any. 3. Detail into Notes/Comments → Comments. 4. Add comment about which grade was removed and the reason (Late No Show/N1 drop). 5. Save out of Comments, STNC, SACD, and STAC.

RGCS (skip if grade has not yet been assigned): 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Delete grade. 3. Click Save All.

RGSD: Backdate to first day of class.

RGN/SRGC: Follow the same fields as On Time No Show — use NS or N1 as Status Reason.

STAC: 1. Remove any empty drop lines. 2. Update drop time to at least one minute after add time. 3. Leave a comment: Timestamp + "Late No Show". 4. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical form.

Fire Tech Note

For Fire Tech late NS drops after end of course, only William Reardon's signature is needed (per Hung).

docs.title: Grade Changes
docs.text:
What Instructors Cannot Request

Instructors cannot request F, D, or NP to W grade changes. The student must submit an EW or A&G request.

EA Drops After W Deadline

Instructors requesting an EA drop after the withdrawal deadline need a Division Dean's signature before processing.

STAC: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Delete grade. 3. Detail into Notes/Comments. 4. Enter IGC into Notes/Dates. 5. Detail into Comments. 6. Make a detailed comment + timestamp and save twice to get back to SACD. 7. Click Save All.

RGCS: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Delete grade. 3. Click Save All.

STAC: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the course. 2. Enter the new grade. 3. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan to Laserfiche if physical form.

docs.title: Class Transfer
docs.text:
Requirements

  • Must be same length to same length (e.g., 8-week to 8-week; 16-week to 16-week — NOT 16 to 8).
  • Same Start and End Dates.
  • Student signature is needed after census date. If still in Add Authorization period, student does not need to sign.
  • If transferring to a new instructor, the new instructor must initiate the class transfer.
  • A student can transfer from a short-term class to a full-term class (per Hung).
  • Can move a student from an honors class to a non-honors class. Cannot move from non-honors to honors.

docs.title: Before Census
docs.text:
RGN: 1. Enter Student ID and detail into the old course. 2. Set Status to D (Dropped), Status Reason to TR (Transfer). 3. Click Save. 4. Go to an empty line and type in the new section. 5. Detail into new section → set Status Reason to TR. 6. Click Save All.

STAC: 1. Detail into the dropped section. 2. Add TR code to dropped section if needed — note the time. 3. Click Save. 4. Detail into new section. 5. Add TR code to added section — change add time to 1 minute after the class drop.

If online course — email instructor:

"Hello [Instructor], your class transfer request has been completed for [student name] from [old section] to [new section]. Please be sure to enter grades from the student's old section to your section. For assistance, please reach out to DistanceEd@sac.edu."

Instructor resource for viewing grades from a previous section: How do I view grades for inactive or concluded students? (Canvas)

docs.title: After Census
docs.text:
RGSD: Backdate system to the Friday before Census date, then follow the same steps as Before Census.

Danger

Don't forget to return RGSD to current date after processing!

docs.title: Acceptable Reasons for EW
docs.text:
Per Title 5, §55024(e), an EW is acceptable when a student withdraws due to reasons beyond their control, including:

  • Illness in the family where the student is the primary caregiver
  • An incarcerated student released from custody or involuntarily transferred before the end of the term
  • The student is the subject of an immigration action
  • Death of an immediate family member
  • Chronic or acute illness
  • Verifiable accidents
  • Natural disasters directly affecting the student

docs.title: Tips for Determination
docs.text:
• Justification — Make sure the reason falls within the acceptable list.
  • Timeline — Make sure the justification falls within the term or can understandably affect performance.
    • Example: A life event before the semester could have lingering effects.
  • Consider load and modality.

docs.title: Key Rules
docs.text:
• EWs submitted before the end of a section: do not require supporting documentation.
  • EWs submitted after the end of a section: must be submitted with an A&G Petition through a counselor, with supporting documentation; include IGC for coding.
  • Can be requested back to January 2018.
  • CVC students can submit EW requests but must use PDF versions (no SSO access).

docs.title: Processing: NO GRADE
docs.text:
Go directly to STAC:

  1. Find the course that matches the form → drill down.
  2. Insert a line.
  3. Status: Dropped
  4. Date:
    • If before the last day to drop with a W: use today's date or leave the student's original drop date.
    • If after the last day to drop with a W: use the last day to drop with a W (find on SRGD).
  5. Reason: EW
  6. Grade: EW
  7. Add only a timestamp in Notes/Comments (lower right corner).

Screenshot Placeholder

EW no-grade entry screenshot is in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Processing: Class Has a Grade of W
docs.text:
1. Remove grade in RGCS.
  2. STAC → find the course → drill down.
  3. Edit the existing drop line:
    • Reason: EW
    • Grade: EW
    • Notes/Comments: Add "Approved EW" + timestamp.
    • If after section end date: include IGC in Notes/Dates.

Screenshot Placeholder

EW W-grade processing screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: Processing: Class Has a Letter Grade
docs.text:
1. Note the original grade.
  2. Remove grade in RGCS.
  3. STAC → find the course → drill down.
  4. Insert a line:
    • Status: Dropped
    • Date: Before the last day to drop with a W (find on SRGD)
    • Reason: EW
    • Grade: EW
    • Notes/Comments: Timestamp only.
    • If after section end date: include IGC in Notes/Dates and note the original grade (e.g., "F to EW").

Screenshot Placeholder

EW letter grade processing screenshots are in the original Word manual.

docs.title: N1 Drops (Academies)
docs.text: STAC: 1. Detail into the desired section. 2. Insert in Status/Dt/Time/Reason: D / Start Date of Class / 11:59 PM / N1 3. Detail into Notes/Comments → Comments. 4. Timestamp. 5. Write comment (e.g., "N1 Drop per Instructor and W. Reardon"). 6. Save All.

docs.title: Change Academic Program
docs.text: SPRO: 1. Drill into current academic program → change status to I (Inactive) → Save. 2. Add new academic program: - Start Date: start of the term (YYYYFA or YYYYSP) - Input admit status.

docs.title: Change Home Campus
docs.text:
(Check with Financial Aid first)

SPRO: 1. Add end date to the old campus. 2. Add a new campus with a start date.

(Student needs government-issued photo ID)

NAE: 1. Edit name. 2. "Is this a name change?" → Yes 3. "Is this birth or other name change?" → Other

docs.text:
(Student needs SSN card AND government photo ID)

NAE: Change or add SSN.

docs.title: Change from CAP to Regular Student

ASUM: Check for active application and confirm student is regular and has HS diploma.

SPRO: 1. Insert a new line for student type → change from CAP to RGLR. 2. Add a new academic program.

docs.title: Unit Overload Petition
docs.text: When a student wants to exceed the allowed units for the term: 1. Student must complete a Student Overload Petition and meet with a counselor for signature. 2. Once signed, student brings it to Admissions for processing. 3. Input on STPE.

PERC: 1. Set end date to yesterday. 2. Detail into the hold → timestamp → leave comment: "Counselor submitted a probation/dismissal intervention form."

SREP: 3. If the student had a TTPRO, TTPR2, or TTPR3: add Late Dates for the student.

docs.title: Subpoenas
docs.text:
Needs to Be Reviewed for Updates

This procedure may need to be reviewed for any policy updates.

docs.title: 1. Notice to Student
docs.text:
• If a Release of Authorization form is attached to the subpoena, no mailed notice is required.
  • If no release is attached:
    1. Prepare the Letter and Notice to Consumer or Employee.
    2. Place the notice in an envelope and mail to the student using:
      • Certified Mail
      • Return Receipt Requested
    3. Deliver the prepared envelope and certified mail forms to Bee in the Mail Room.
    4. Upon receipt:
      • Attach the returned receipt to the filed subpoena.
      • If the mail is returned, attach the returned mail to the filed subpoena as well.
  • Wait 7 calendar days for a response.
  • If no response is received after 7 days, begin processing the subpoena.

docs.title: 2. Review and Distribution
docs.text:
1. Review the subpoena to determine what records are being requested.
  2. Forward the subpoena to all applicable departments to gather the required information.
  3. Compile all received documentation.
  4. Send completed records and documents to Risk Management.

docs.title: A. Application Records
docs.text:
• LaserFiche — Print the student's application.
  • OpenCCCApply Report Center — Print the student's application.

docs.title: B. Transcripts (TRAN)
docs.text:
1. Enter the Student ID.
  2. Save.
  3. P → FormFusion
  4. Use the printing code.
  5. Save and update.

docs.title: C. Other Student Records (If Requested)
docs.text: Copy all relevant correspondence and student records including instructor rosters, grades, and programs. Ensure all documents are in order and legible. Only include the requested student's records. Redact or white out other students' information as needed.

docs.title: D. Full Grades and Rosters (If Required)
docs.text:
1. STAC — Print course list.
  2. M: Drive → Instructor Grades:
    • Search by semester.
    • Search by instructor and section.
    • Locate rosters for each registered class.
  3. Redact or black out all other students' information.
  4. Convert all documents to PDF and ensure redactions are applied correctly.

docs.title: 4. Subpoenas Requiring Financial Information
docs.text:
Contact the appropriate offices for financial records:

  • CO/Auxiliary Services — Jennie Adams, Ext. 46433
  • Financial Aid — Thuy Huong Nguyen, Ext. 46485

Email the department(s) with the subpoena request and student name/ID. Request that documentation be sent via interoffice mail if applicable.

docs.title: 5. Subpoena Fees Form
docs.text:
• Track all time spent processing the subpoena for billing purposes.
  • Copy the tracked time from Excel and paste it as an image into a letterhead document.
  • Save the file as Subpoena Fees.
  • Convert the document to PDF by right-clicking on the file.

docs.title: 6. Custodian of Records Form
docs.text:
• Complete the Custodian of Records form provided by HR.
  • Attach the completed form to the subpoena package.

docs.title: Document Order
docs.text:
1. Custodian of Records Form
  2. Subpoena Fees Form
  3. Application
  4. Transcripts
  5. Additional subpoena-requested documents

docs.title: Distribution and Filing
docs.text:
• Make copies of all documents.
  • Email the completed subpoena package to Annina Brown.
  • File physical copies in the first drawer located by Mark's office.
  • Include the notification letter to the student in the file.
    • If the letter is returned, file it with the subpoena documentation.

docs.title: 8. Special Notes and Exceptions
docs.text:
Public Defender Subpoenas

Do not pay the $15 fee.

Workers' Compensation Subpoenas

  • Include application and transcripts only.
  • Send documents to the District.
  • No fee letter is required.
  • Print official transcripts from TRAN.

docs.title: Process Summary
docs.text:
1. Check the Evaluation Flowchart.
    • Do not evaluate for any SCC Academic Programs (1/14/25 TP).
    • If student has both AA/AS and AAT/AST: evaluate for Plan A and Plan B (1/14/25 TP).
    • Plan C by request only (1/9/25 TP).
  2. Determine what GE areas are already completed using PSPR (Program: SAC.LA.AA, Catalog: 2010-2011) → drill into "Evaluate" → Save All → Download → Open file.
  3. Check Assist.org to find GE areas for the student's courses.
  4. Fill in GE area gaps via Colleague.
  5. Update IASU and Transcript Log when complete.

docs.title: GE Processing — EXTS
docs.text: Same steps as Pseudo Processing above, then detail into EQUIV and delete entries that don't apply.

docs.title: GE Processing — STAC
docs.text:
1. Detail into each course entered.
  2. Select SACD → OK.
  3. Enter:
    • Name: Course name from transfer institution.
    • Title: GE Area + institution name (use = to preserve case): "=Plan A - E1 Fullerton"
    • Term: Term of the transfer course (YYYYSU / YYYYFA / YYYYSI / YYYYSP).
  4. Repeat for all transfer course areas.

docs.title: CVC Students Needing to Rebill as SAC Students
docs.text:
STAC: Note when the student registered.

RGSD: Update to the date of registration.

RGN: 1. Drop the student from the course. 2. Re-register. 3. Check STAC to verify the times of drops and adds make sense — otherwise the rebill will not work.

SPRO: 1. Make sure the CVC program is turned off. 2. If needed, push dates back so the CVC program is off before registration, and the SAC program is active by the time of registration.

Cashier's: Ask for rebill.

docs.title: Cashier Remove Drop — Rebill Request
docs.text:
Cashier may request removing a drop so they can rebill (to have certain fee waivers apply), before reinstating the W drop. This only applies to W drops.

STAC: 1. Take screenshot of all classes and identify the class. 2. Take screenshot of detail view showing when it was dropped (date and time). 3. Delete the drop line (if applicable). 4. Remove W or grade.

RGCS: Remove W or grade.

Let Cashier's know to rebill.

Once rebill is complete:

STAC: Update the time and re-enter W or grade.

RGCS: Verify W or grade is showing.

Note

No need to file anything — no true changes were made to the record.

docs.title: Class Transfer (AR Tech)
docs.text:
Applies to both individual transfers and division-requested bulk transfers for class cancellations.

Warning

Must be same length to same length (e.g., 8-week to 8-week; 16-week to 16-week).

docs.title: Class Transfer Before Census
docs.text:
RGN:

  1. Enter Student ID and detail into the old course.
  2. Update Status to D (Dropped).
  3. Backdate to before Census so the student does not get double-charged.
  4. Update Status Reason to TR.
  5. Click Save.
  6. Go to the last line and type in the new section.
  7. Detail into the new section.
  8. Update Status Reason to TR.
  9. Click Save All.

STAC:

  1. Detail into the dropped section.
  2. Add TR code to the dropped section — note the time.
  3. Click Save.
  4. Detail into the new section.
  5. Add TR code to the added section — change add time to 1 minute after the class drop.

If online course: Email DistanceEd@sac.edu to move Canvas submissions from old section to new section.

docs.title: Class Transfer After Census
docs.text:
RGSD: Backdate system to the Friday before Census date, then follow all steps above.

Danger

Don't forget to return RGSD to current date after processing!

docs.title: Error — Reversing a Transfer
docs.text:
STAC: Note the class section that was dropped in error.

RGN: Drop the added section and re-register in the prior section.

STAC:

  1. Add ER (Error) code to the newly dropped/wrong section and add a comment explaining why it was added then dropped. Be specific. Timestamp.
  2. Drill into the class that was added back — delete the add and drop line so only the original registration information remains.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
1. Division provides Waitlist Roster (AM118 report).
  2. Ensure there is space in the desired section.
  3. Batch Registration — verify eligibility first (run on No Update first).
  4. Drop students from their old waitlists.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
SWLM or Query — Get students in requested sections:

  1. Go to the section of students to be moved.
  2. Export the Excel sheet.

SLED: Put students in SLED list.

SCBL:

  1. Name your Section Block (will be used in BRGN).
  2. Enter Term/Start/End Date.
  3. Enter course name and section number.

BRGN:

Field Value Section Block Your SCBL name Reg User Group WEBREG Date Today's date Update Test with N first, then Y

Remove any students with errors from the list before running with Y.

SWLM: Drop students who were registered from their waitlist.

Final Steps:

  1. Send email with Excel sheet of registered/non-registered students.
  2. File email and processed waitlist roster to student records in Laserfiche.

docs.title: Waitlist Rollover WLAV — After Term Start (Manual Daily)
docs.text:
The savelist is generated by ITS in SLCR and run in the background nightly.

  • WLAV.SL — Use if the session you are running for has already started. Holds students that need to be rolled over for sections within that term that have not yet started.

If the Division needs an individual WL rollover for one section during the day:

  • Remove the Savelist name.
  • Add the section number.
  • Run.

docs.title: Before Section Start
docs.text: If the department is going to cancel the section anyway — have them just cancel the section, then batch register once students are deregistered from the cancellation.

docs.title: After Section Start — Run Batch Transfer Process
docs.text:
Batch Drops:

SLED — Load Course.Section.IDs via DREG Query.

DREG — Run drop.

Batch Enroll:

SLED — New SLED list with true Student IDs.

SCBL — Use previously created list. Enter class name and section number. (Multiple sections can be enrolled at the same time only if exact same roster AND same section dates.)

BRGN:

Field Value Section Blocks Name used in SCBL RG User Group ARSTAFF Date Today (backdate for CJ if needed) Update No first to test — essential to get registration errors Saved List Name Name used in SLED

Critical errors requiring student removal from SLED:

  • Require Overload Petition
  • Repeat — does not meet requirements
  • Does not meet prereq
  • Time conflict

Run again with Update: Yes to actually enroll.

STAC: Add TR coding to each record. Check that enroll time is after drop time.

docs.title: Why Track Semester by Semester
docs.text:
Tracking students in special programs on a semester-by-semester basis ensures:

  • Accurate reporting — meets funding, compliance, and audit requirements.
  • Flexibility — accurately reflects students who leave and rejoin programs.
  • Funding alignment — many grants are tied to term-based participation.
  • Consistency — aligns with course enrollment and other institutional processes.
  • Historical insight — supports grant applications and accreditation reviews.

Tip

Keep an internal department Excel sheet tracking your students to upload into CORM for campus-wide tracking. Each semester, upload current students into the current term.

docs.title: Excessive Absence Drops
docs.text:
Info

EA Drops occur between the Census date and the last day to drop with a "W". For late EA drop requests, email faculty that the deadline has passed; faculty must get Division Dean permission before requesting.

RGN: Find the course and detail in.

RGCS:

Field Value Status/Date D (Dropped) — defaults to today Status Reason EA (Excessive Absence) Drop Grade W Last Attended Date Request date of the form (leave if already filled)

Click Save All. If corequisite or higher-level course exists, drop it too using reason PD.

STAC: 1. Detail into course. 2. Check for empty drop lines using the ">" arrow — delete any. 3. Update Grade to W. 4. Click Save All.

Error — Class active in STAC but not showing on RGN: 1. Check in STAC if the program is CC or CE. 2. If CC, check RGCS for a grade entered in error.

docs.title: On Time No Show Drops
docs.text:
Verify before processing: - Submitted by instructor (from instructor/division email or Dynamic Forms). - Submitted prior to class drop deadline. - Form is complete (including section number).

NS vs N1

  • NS = initiated by faculty submitting a late No Show.
  • N1 = special circumstances (VP of Academic Affairs, Dean, student, etc.).
  • NS is considered late if submitted on or after census — requires Division Dean and VP signature.
  • If student added after census: push registration back to first day of class and drop. Add notes in STAC documenting original registration date and time.

RGSD: Backdate to first day of class.

RGN: Find course and detail in.

RGCS:

Field Value Status/Date D — defaults to RGSD date Status Reason NS Drop Grade Remove anything Last Attended Date Remove anything Never Attended Y

Click Save All. Drop corequisite/higher-level course with PD if applicable.

STAC: 1. Update Reason to NS. 2. Check for empty drop lines — delete any. 3. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan if physical form.

docs.title: If Student Dropped Before You Can Process
docs.text:
STAC: 1. Update drop date to first day of class. 2. Update Reason to NS. 3. Remove W or grade. 4. Click Save All.

RGCS: 1. Update Status Reason to NS if not already. 2. Remove W or grade. 3. Update Never Attended to Y. 4. Click Save All.

docs.title: Late NS/N1 Drops When Grade Is on File
docs.text:
Info

For open entry/open exit sections: backdate the student's registration to the day they added the course and drop them on that day.

STAC (skip if no grade yet): 1. Detail into Notes/Comments → Comments. 2. Add comment about which grade was removed and the reason. 3. Delete grade. 4. Save out of Comments, STNC, SACD, and STAC.

RGCS (skip if no grade yet): 1. Delete grade. 2. Click Save All.

RGSD: Backdate to first day of class.

RGN/RGCS: Follow same fields as On Time NS — use NS or N1 as Status Reason.

Note

For N1 drops: leave Never Attended blank (unlike NS where it is Y).

STAC: 1. Update Reason to NS or N1. 2. Remove empty drop lines. 3. Click Save All.

Laserfiche: Scan if physical form.

docs.title: Batch Drops
docs.text:
SLED — Load data.

DREG (requires Course.Section.ID from BGVU query): 1. First run Update Mode "No" — review for errors. 2. Reason: Transfer. 3. Cannot backdate a batch drop. 4. If all looks good, run again with Update Mode "Yes".

docs.title: Student Conduct Drops
docs.text:
N1 Drop: Less common — requires backdating to an NS drop for no transcript entry and refund. Does not require Dr. Lamb's signature for Student Conduct Drops (per Liz).

N2 Drop (Disciplinary): Most common — results in a W grade. For past classes, backdate to prior to the W deadline.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
SRGD: Note the day prior to the W deadline for the section.

RGSD: - If N1: backdate to first day of class. - If N2: backdate to prior to W deadline.

If grade already assigned:

STAC → detail into class → remove grade. RGCS → detail into class → remove grade.

RGN: Find course → change status to "Dropped."

RGCS: 1. Use Instructor Request Date as the drop date. 2. Status Reason: N1 or N2. 3. Drop Grade: W if N2. 4. Drop higher-level course if tied, using reason PD.

STAC: 1. Check drop lines and remove duplicates. 2. Enter W grade (also auto-applied in BGVU). 3. Add detailed comment with timestamp. - Example: "Drop request received from student conduct office. Form filed to LF for details."

docs.title: Prerequisite Drops
docs.text:
RGVE — Run prerequisite check.

Note

This report takes a long time. Run it and retrieve from UTFB (Directory File Name: _HOLD_, Security Type: PB, Item Names: RGVE...). Save as .csv and open with Excel.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
1. First run (Deregister Failed Records = NO) — generates suggestions for deregistration. Review list for accuracy. Load into SLED.
  2. Second run — actually deregisters, referencing the SLED list.

docs.title: RGVE Setup
docs.text:
• Set "Deregister Failed Records" to NO for first run.
  • Update Terms to the term you are checking.

docs.title: Filtering the Report
docs.text:
• Error Message: Search for "The following..."
  • Requisite Waiver Provider (Name): Show only Blanks.
  • Deregister?: Show only Yes.

docs.title: Checking
docs.text: • Verify students should actually be dropped.

docs.title: Query
docs.text:
• Query for STUDENT_COURSE_SEC_ID using RGVE.sql.
  • After running, double-check results — if a long list, verify section number matches the correct student. Run a match on Student IDs from the query against the drop list. Highlight multiples.

docs.title: Deregister
docs.text:
• Create a savedlist in SLED of students to be dropped.
  • Use that savedlist in RGVE with Deregister = YES.
  • Download report and verify students are dropped. If "No" — check the override. If A&R staff override, manually drop for PD.

docs.title: Email Students
docs.text:
After RGVE, run PCEX with student communication code PREREQ and Y in "Process Letter Request."

To view the PREREQ email: go to DOC → type PREREQ → detail into "Document Paragraphs."

docs.title: Determining Good vs. Bad ID
docs.text:
• If Financial Aid was awarded, that record must stay as Good ID (check AIDE and AWSU — verify with Thuy Huong Nguyen and Ha Dinh personally, not the FinAid email).
  • If a duplicate was created by HR, the HR record always must stay as Good ID.
  • If neither applies: take the record with more data as Good ID.

Tip

Work with two Colleague screens open on two monitors — one for BAD ID, one for GOOD ID. Use color coding to keep them straight.

  • Only move College Credit records. Contact SCE to move non-credit.
  • If MM is on the bad ID, reach out to Assessment Center to have it moved.
  • Always move Applications (ASUM) first, then classes (STAC).
  • ALL classes must be moved including dropped without grade, dropped prior to class start, cancelled, and enrollment errors.

Active Classes on Bad ID

Work closely with ITS and Distance Ed to have Canvas access moved, restored, and tested on Good ID right away. Inform the student of their new login info immediately — call rather than email, then follow up with details by email.

docs.title: 1. Move Personal Information (NAE)
docs.text:
1. Verify all information on Good ID is correct. If the newest application was moved from Bad ID and there is discrepant info on NAE, access the application on CCCReport Center and update personal data accordingly.
  2. Move SSN from Bad ID to Good ID → Save All.

This step verifies it is indeed a duplicate before moving any other data.

docs.title: 2. Move ASUM
docs.text: Add new entry, then in SHAP: - Change Catalog year first. - Duplicate all entries including dates, times, and processing clerks (ADW, MS, etc.). - Add comment in ASUM: "Application for SAC.XXX.XX for Term XXX was moved from BAD ID XXXXXX to GOOD ID XXXXXX." + timestamp.

docs.title: 3. Update Dates on SPRO
docs.text: Check programs newly added on SPRO and copy activation dates from Bad ID to Good ID. Start with activating the oldest academic program and keep it active to make registering courses easier, then add all details at the end.

docs.title: 4. Check Residency
docs.text:
Verify the newly moved applications haven't changed residency status. Confirm any later residency reclassification or AB540 processed on Good ID is still intact.

If loading CAPU: Check STPE and duplicate/enter non-resident waivers for terms if needed.

docs.title: 5. Move STAC Records
docs.text:
Work one class at a time — on the old ID, the pointer will automatically move to the next record when you cancel out.

Add class on Good ID:

RGSD: Backdate to individual class registration date.

RGN: - Add class. - If error "doesn't meet eligibility": check STPE for CAP waiver. - If course is too old and has no section number: add directly through STAC.

STAC: 1. Update time to true reg time (if drops exist, ensure add times are after drop time). 2. Check for additional lines (drops). 3. Enter grade. 4. Add comment: "This section has been moved from BAD ID XXX to GOOD ID XXX." + timestamp. 5. Move the CSU/IGETC/AA. 6. Check STRP — if repeat, update on new record as well.

RGSD: Update back to today.

Drop class on Bad ID:

RGSD: Set date to drop date (first day of class).

STAC & RGCS: Remove grades.

RGN: Drop at first day of class. (If added during ADD period (status A), drop with add date.)

STAC: 1. Enter ER (Error) code next to the drop. 2. Verify only 1 drop line was added. 3. Add comment: "This section has been moved from BAD ID XXX to GOOD ID XXX." + timestamp.

RGSD: Update back to today.

docs.title: 6. Move Laserfiche Records
docs.text: Laserfiche: Move all documents from Bad ID to Good ID record.

docs.title: 7. DADD and Canvas Access
docs.text: DADD: 1. Move all records from Bad ID to Good ID. 2. After move, delete any CCID / DCCID or OLD CCID from the Bad ID to avoid Canvas issues. 3. Contact Distance Education (Jimmy or Ambar) to verify Canvas account is created and active for permanent ID. If not, open an ITS helpdesk ticket to Michael Ward.

docs.title: 8. Set Bad ID to Duplicate
docs.text:
BIO: 1. Add =XXX before last name. 2. Remove middle name and copy Good ID into Middle Name field. 3. Set Privacy to X (Duplicate). 4. Set Directory to N.

SPRO: Inactivate all programs with first day of term.

PERC: 1. Enter 1 ADM Hold. 2. Drill into Comments → add: "Duplicate ID — DO NOT USE. Good ID XXXXXXX — [clerk name who found duplicate]." + timestamp.

Notify Cashier's and any other relevant parties.

Give back to ARII to file and move Laserfiche records.

docs.title: Unmerging Wrongly Merged Applications
docs.text:
Do Not Delay

Original data in Test/UAT could get cloned and lost. Act immediately.

  1. Check the original student record in TEST to identify what belongs to the original student.
  2. Load the new application from CCCApply (name search if prior record exists; otherwise create new student record).
  3. If SSN got merged: remove from merged ID first, then apply to new account.
  4. Once new student application is correctly loaded, clean up the merged account:
  5. NAE: Update address, delete wrong address from history. Check phone, email, SSN, ethnicity.
  6. SPRO: Inactivate program with first day of term. Add comment. Check ASPR and Student type.
  7. ASUM: Set application to AD (Do Not Use) and add comment.
  8. DADD: Remove any added CCCID from the wrong application.
  9. STAC: Move already-registered classes from wrong student to correct ID and delete from merged record.

docs.title: Canvas Access and Duplicate Scenarios (ITS Notes)
Case 1 — Different Students, Wrong CCCID on Existing Student
docs.text: Canvas SIS import fails for new student due to duplicate CCCID. Search NAE → DADD. Open ITS Help Desk Ticket for Canvas Admin to update existing student's CCCID.

docs.title: Case 2 — Different Students, Wrong CCCID on New Student
docs.text: Same as Case 1 but A&R corrects the CCCID in Colleague for the new student — no ITS action needed.

docs.title: Case 3 — Same Person, Existing Student Has Duplicate CCCID
docs.text: Mark existing (Bad) record as duplicate. Open ITS Help Desk Ticket. Canvas Admin updates CCCID for Bad record, then merges Canvas accounts.

docs.title: Case 4 — Same Person, New Student Has Incorrect CCCID
docs.text: Mark new (Bad) record as duplicate. No ITS Canvas action needed.

docs.title: Case 5 — Same Person, Different CCCIDs
docs.text: Both Canvas accounts may exist. Search using ;PERSON.EMAIL.ADDRESSES, ;SSN, or ;BIRTH.DATE. Open ITS Help Desk Ticket for Canvas Admin to merge users regardless of which record is duplicate.

docs.title: Case 6 — Different Students, CCCID Update Causes Conflict
docs.text:
Open ITS Help Desk Ticket to correct — even if no login issues, updates may not flow to Canvas correctly.

Search format for DADD lookup

;PERSON.ALT.IDS ABC1234

docs.title: Unprocessing a Form
docs.text:
To bring a form back from Processed status:

  1. Mark as Unprocessed.
  2. Open PDF.
  3. Select Actions.
  4. Select Return For Revision.

docs.title: Reopening a Deleted Form
docs.text:
You can reopen forms by deleting and then un-deleting them:

  1. In Dynamic Forms, go to the processed form → under Action, click Delete.
  2. Go to the Deleted tab.
  3. Find your form → under Action, click Un-Delete.
  4. Go to the Multi Pending tab.
  5. The form should be back in Multi-Pending status, ready for you to Complete Form.

docs.title: Form Getting Cut Off on Sides in PDFs
docs.text:
• Update the padding on the sides to push everything into the middle.
  • This only updates new forms from that point forward.

docs.title: Day Prior to Event
docs.text:
1. Bulk assign 1EDPR for CORM with start date set to day of ED participation.
  2. Assign Registration Dates for the relevant ED date.

Check the list for: - Active Application - Majors - Home Campus - Student Type - Residency Status

Process: - In the ID column edit for query with formula: =CHAR(39) & A1 & CHAR(39) & CHAR(44) - Run ED Query to check the above fields.

Note: The query may list duplicate Student IDs if students have multiple programs — highlight duplicates.

Student Type: - Verify or update all CAP to RGLR with start date 06/01, if a SU/FA or earlier application with RGLR student type is on record. - If no RGLR application: let Outreach know. - Compare "Application" vs. "SPRO" student types from Query. If APP is RGLR but SPRO shows CAP, update on SPRO.

Home Campus: - Add SAC for all CEC students. - If SCC home campus: let Outreach know.

Residency: - Fix all CERs (check if application status is R before fixing). - Highlight Non-Res and Foreign → let Outreach know.

Major: - Highlight all majors that don't qualify for FinAid (all certificate majors) → let Outreach know.

Run modified Applications Query to check if all students have an active application status (remove duplicates, use MATCH formula).

docs.title: Morning of Event
docs.text: Outreach sends additional students: - Assign registration dates. - Add to 1EDPR on CORM with relevant start date.

docs.title: During Event
docs.text:
Assist with student issues (missing application, residency, etc.).

Warning

Make sure to add any onsite additions to 1EDPR and assign reg date on site.

Assign CORM flags: - Puente: 1PUEN - Ulink: 1ULIN

When assigning these, set to Active and assign role "Active".

docs.title: Honors Waivers (ULINK Honors Cohort)
docs.text:
If counseling is not available, A&R must enter the Honors Waiver:

PERC: - Enter general HONRS waiver. - Start date: today. - End date: prior to next term registration start (Fall Honors end date: 10/31/YYYY).

XSRWS: - For any classes with prerequisites (e.g., ENGL 101), also place a waiver on XSRWS for Honors classes. - Drill in to complete.

Once waivers are placed, have students register and verify completion via RGN.

docs.title: Confirm Registration
docs.text:
(Starting from 2nd week of ED — for first week, start at Registration Reporting below.)

  1. Run CORM Flag for 1EDPR by event date.
  2. Compare (MATCH) with the preliminary list for that day.
  3. Run CORM Flag for 1EDPR by student IDs who were on the preliminary list but don't appear in the CORM report (they may have an earlier CORM start date).
  4. Change CORM start date for those students to that day, then proceed.

docs.title: Registration Reporting
docs.text:
1. Run CORM Query to extract all students added with start date = event date from 1EDPR.
  2. Formula: =CHAR(39) & A1 & CHAR(39) & CHAR(44)
  3. Use that student list to run Registration Query for ED students.
  4. Identify students on 1EDPR who did not register (MATCH formula).
  5. Send both lists to Outreach:
  6. Total expected students with non-registered highlighted (possible no-shows).
  7. Registration report with total units registered.
  8. Copy Hung on this report.
  9. Add registration report to master list Excel file.
  10. Add 1EADE for students who registered for Fall.

docs.title: Program Deactivation
docs.text:
Academic program deactivation is a multi-step process run quarterly.

See Hung's detailed Program Deactivation documentation for the full step-by-step procedure. The steps below are a summary.

docs.title: Step ONE — Identify Programs to Deactivate
docs.text: Identify programs that are no longer active and need to be deactivated in Colleague.

docs.title: Step TWO — Prepare Student Records
docs.text: Review student records currently in the programs to be deactivated. Ensure students are moved to active programs before deactivation.

docs.title: Step THREE — SPRO Updates
docs.text: Inactivate the academic programs on SPRO with appropriate end dates.

docs.title: Step FOUR — Verify
docs.text: Verify all student records have been updated and no active students remain in the deactivated program.

docs.title: Step FIVE — Final Cleanup
docs.text: Complete any remaining cleanup steps as outlined in Hung's documentation.

docs.title: Step SIX — RLDE
docs.text: Update any RLDE rules that reference the deactivated programs.

docs.title: Step SEVEN — Communication
docs.text: Notify relevant departments of the program deactivation.

docs.title: Step EIGHT — Documentation
docs.text:
Document the deactivation in the appropriate logs.

Note

The full detailed procedure is in Hung's Program Deactivation documentation.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Course families = a group of courses that teach similar skills at different levels (e.g., beginning to advanced painting).
  • You can only take 4 total courses per family (or 6 in quarters).
  • It does not matter if you fail, pass, or withdraw — they all count toward the limit.
  • Some courses can be repeatable by regulation (e.g., for transfer), but the total still cannot exceed 4 in the family.
  • Districts must justify the distinction between courses with clear differences in student learning outcomes.
  • This rule exists to promote fairness and limit course hoarding.
